item.title: Tour Down Under Stage 1: Henley Beach to Gumeracha
item.description: Stage 1 has often suited the pure sprinters, but next year the punchy all-rounders could have their chance. The stage runs from Henley Beach to Gumeracha over 164.9 km. It includes the first-ever crossing of Birkenhead Bridge via historic Port Adelaide, sprint points in Williamstown and Lobethal, and three ascents of Checker Hill.

item.title: Stay protected from the heat during #TDF2026
item.description: The #TDF2026 passes by, but the heat remains. Protect yourself before the riders pass, during the stage, and after the finish. Seek shade whenever possible, wear a bucket hat or hat, drink water regularly even if you do not feel thirsty, and keep an eye on children, older people, and the most vulnerable people around you. In an emergency, call 18 or 112 immediately. Enjoy the Tour festivities, but avoid heatstroke. On the Tour, protection is also a team effort.

item.title: Behind every champion, there is a team. The Department of Hauts-de-Seine, an official Tour de France partner since 2022, sponsors the Best Teammate Award.
item.description: Behind every champion, there is a team. The Department of Hauts-de-Seine, an official Tour de France partner since 2022, sponsors the Best Teammate Award, which recognizes those who use their talent for the good of the team. Each week, Tour fans are invited to vote for the rider who stood out for sacrifice, generosity, and commitment to their team. Through this award, the Department of Hauts-de-Seine highlights an essential sporting value: team spirit. In your opinion, who will be the best teammate of #TDF2026?

item.title: Tour de France x Rexel France: a series on the roads of electrification.
item.description: Tour de France x Rexel France: a series on the roads of electrification. At every stage of #TDF2026, something happens that viewers do not see on television. Along the route, local authorities, businesses, craftspeople, and experts are driving the energy transition with concrete projects that are already changing daily life for French people. With our new series, “Vous êtes au courant ?”, we meet them to tell this other race: the electrification of France. An 8-episode series available now on Courant Positif, the electrification media. 👉 https://entreprise.rexel.fr/courant-positif…

item.title: Tour de France 2026: Heat and crowds require maximum caution
item.description: On the route across our department, the passage of the caravan and the riders is a major moment, but we must remain careful, especially with an orange heatwave alert. Key reminders: I plan my travel in advance, as some roads are closed; I do not stand at the exit of a corner; I do not cross the road as the caravan and riders approach; I keep my animals on a leash; I do not use my drone; I do not run alongside the riders. In hot weather: I drink water regularly; I protect myself from the sun with a hat, sunscreen, and suitable clothing; I avoid prolonged exposure, especially during the hottest hours; I stay attentive to vulnerable people around me. Cheer, encourage, enjoy: yes. Put riders or spectators in danger: no. — @Prefet64 Jul 7, 2026

Discover the teams set to battle for stage victories and the coveted green jersey!

item.title: KM 92 with @hautsdeseinefr: what do you do as a teammate?
item.description: Your team has riders up front: what do you do as a teammate? 1. Let the other teams work and stay with your leader. 2. Attack again to join them. 3. Wait. Reply in the comments. A draw will take place after the stage. The Département des #HautsDeSeine, a Tour de France partner, highlights the unsung heroes with the Best Teammate Award.

item.title: Want to be part of Britain’s biggest professional cycle race from the inside?
item.description: The Lloyds Tour of Britain Women needs you. The event runs from Wednesday 19 August to Sunday 23 August and will pass through Cumbria, Lancashire, North Wales, Central Wales and Warwickshire. If you are interested, please go to the British Cycling website and fill out the application form before this Sunday: https://forms.cloud.microsoft/pages/responsepage.aspx…

item.title: TDF2026 biodiversity spotlight at the Massif de Saint-Barthélemy nature reserve
item.description: The Massif de Saint-Barthélemy regional nature reserve is an open-air laboratory for studying the effects of climate change. Using the CIMAe protocol, scientists standardize their observations to better understand how wetlands and the species that live there are changing, so that conservation actions can be adapted to the challenges of climate change. Video — @LeTour Jul 7, 2026

item.title: TDF2026: protect yourself from the heat
item.description: The #TDF2026 passes, but the heat remains. Protect yourself before the riders pass, during the stage, and after the finish: seek shade whenever possible, wear a bucket hat or hat, drink water regularly even if you do not feel thirsty, and keep an eye on children, older people, and the most vulnerable people around you. In an emergency, call 18 or 112 immediately. Enjoy the Tour festivities, yes. Heatstroke, no. On the Tour, protection is also a team sport!

item.title: Five years on, Wout van Aert's Mont Ventoux victory still stands out
item.description: Five years on, that unforgettable triumph on Mont Ventoux still stands out as one of the Tour’s greatest recent moments. Five years ago, Wout van Aert conquered the Giant of Provence. It remains one of the most iconic victories of recent years. #TDF2026 — @LeTour Jul 7, 2026

item.title: Shimano neutral service helped riders cope with the heat
item.description: Thanks to the Shimano neutral service, the riders were able to cope with the heat. ☀️🚴 💧 149 bottles distributed 🧊 12 ice socks provided ⚡ 5 energy gels handed out Vital support throughout the stage. 💛 Grâce aux équipes d’assistance neutre @shimanoroad, les coureurs ont pu faire face à la chaleur. ☀️🚴 💧 149 bidons distribués 🧊 12 chaussettes glacées ⚡ 5 gels énergétiques Un soutien précieux tout au long de l’étape. 💛 #TDF2026

item.title: From Gdynia on the Baltic to Wieliczka near the Tatras
item.description: In August, the 83rd Tour de Pologne will cross Poland from north to south, and we will travel with the peloton. This year our convoy will grow with three new vehicles: a bus and two passenger cars. These technical vehicles will support our drivers and help secure the equipment on the road. Each one has a special livery prepared for the occasion. The design is fully original, from the first sketch to the final print, created by Graphic Designer Oskar Jażdż. If you see our convoy during the race, come over — we will have gifts for you. Share your thoughts on the livery.

item.title: Countdown to the 83rd Tour de Pologne begins
item.description: We are proud to announce that we are the Official Insurer and Mountain Classification Sponsor of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour, the biggest and most prestigious cycling race in Poland. Starting August 3, you can follow the battle of the world’s best riders with us on this year’s route. We are delighted to be part of this special event for the sixth year in a row and to support top-level sporting excitement.

item.title: Countdown to the 83rd Tour de Pologne
item.description: We are counting down to the 83rd Tour de Pologne. We are proud to announce that we are the Official Insurer and the Mountains Classification sponsor of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour, the biggest and most prestigious cycling race in Poland. From 3 August, you can follow the best riders in the world as they compete on this year’s route.

item.title: Yannis Voisard is in top form
item.description: Finishing in the top 20 at the Tour de Romandie is a strong result, and Yannis Voisard has already done it twice. Making the top 20 at the Tour de France is even more impressive. The rider from Jura, racing for Tudor Pro Cycling Team, continues to impress.

item.title: The 2027 Santos Tour Down Under will be the longest edition on record
item.description: Our world-first format change is just the start. The 2027 Santos Tour Down Under will also be the longest edition on record, with 876 kilometres across the men’s race and 397 kilometres for the women. That means more time to see top international riders in action, and fresh challenges across stage routes designed to test even the toughest competitors.

item.title: The 2027 Santos Tour Down Under will be the longest edition on record
item.description: The 2027 Santos Tour Down Under will feature a new format and be the longest edition on record, with 876 kilometres in the men’s race and 397 kilometres in the women’s race. The longer route will give fans more chances to see top international riders in action and will bring new challenges across stage routes designed to test even the toughest competitors. What does 1273 add up to? Swipe to find out. #TourDownUnder

item.title: The highs and lows of the Tour de France
item.description: An impressive Lennert Van Eetvelt finished 6th on the first mountain stage in the Pyrenees, marking his first career top-10 in the Tour. Arnaud De Lie was unable to continue. Despite the difficult days, Arnaud showed combativity and optimism and handled the heat and demanding climbs as best he could. It is a setback for the team, but we remain upbeat and focused on reaching our goals. #RisingTogether 📸 @cyclingmedia_agency @photonewsbelgium

item.title: Safety first
item.description: “My mom texted me last night to say that, unfortunately, we wouldn’t be able to see each other today because of the rules in place today. It’s too bad my parents couldn’t watch the Tour pass by today, but anything that improves safety needs to be put in place.” 🎙️ #TDF2026

item.title: Get well soon, Arnaud
item.description: Get well soon, Arnaud. Lotto-Intermarché says illness forced Arnaud De Lie to withdraw from the Tour de France during stage 3. He had been recovering from an abdominal infection before the race, but the heat and the demands of the stage proved too much. After being dropped, he tried to stay in the race with help from teammate Baptiste Veistroffer, but eventually stopped on the final climb to protect his health. Arnaud De Lie said it was a huge disappointment and that he will now focus on recovering and returning stronger later in the season.

item.title: Tour de France 2026: exceptional measures to protect the population
item.description: Tour de France 2026: exceptional measures to protect the population. Due to the wildfire situation in the Pyrénées-Orientales, the prefect has issued an order changing the conditions for stage 3 of the Tour de France. The order prohibits the public from going near or along the route, as well as to the finish area. The publicity caravan is cancelled. Only the riders, vehicles essential to the organization, and security and emergency services are allowed on the route. Compliance with this order is mandatory. Going onto the route or into prohibited areas is an offence and may result in penalties under the regulations. These exceptional measures are intended to allow emergency services to be fully mobilized to fight the fires, ensure the safety of residents, and avoid disrupting ongoing operations. For the full order, visit: https://www.pyrenees-orientales.gouv.fr/…/Recueil_06_07_202…

item.title: Protect the Tour’s landscapes
item.description: The Tour is about the beauty of our landscapes, so let’s preserve them. In hot weather, a cigarette butt can start a fire. One simple action is enough: keep your cigarette butts, use a pocket ashtray, and take your waste with you. Stay alert. Together, let’s protect the Tour’s regions. #TDF2026

item.title: Biodiversity at Tour de France 2026, Stage 3
item.description: In the Pyrénées Catalanes Natural Park in Eyne, the campaign “Que la montagne est belle,” launched in 2021, encourages everyone to adopt the right habits to help preserve natural spaces. This initiative offers a new way to raise awareness about biodiversity.

item.title: Stage 3 of the #TDF2026: no spectators in the final 40 kilometers or at the finish area.
item.description: Stage 3 of the #TDF2026: no spectators along the road in the final 40 kilometers or at the finish area. Due to the wildfire currently affecting the Pyrénées-Orientales, and to allow emergency services to be fully mobilized, the 3rd stage of the Tour de France will take place without spectators or a publicity caravan on French territory.

item.title: Stage 3 of the #TDF2026: no spectators in the final 40 kilometers or at the finish area.

Due to the wildfire currently affecting the Pyrénées-Orientales, and to allow emergency services to be fully mobilized, the 3rd stage of the Tour de France will take place without spectators or a publicity caravan on French territory.

item.title: Tour de France 2026 Stage 3: No spectators in the final 40 kilometers or at the finish area
item.description: Due to the wildfire currently affecting the Pyrénées-Orientales and to allow emergency services to be fully mobilized, Stage 3 of the Tour de France will take place without spectators and without the publicity caravan on French territory.

item.title: Made in South Australia. Made for the Santos Tour Down Under.
item.description: From being a spectator at every stage of the Santos Tour Down Under as a child, thanks to Mum, to riding in the race and winning the Health Partners Rider of the Day, Tristan Saundo shows that local South Australians can reach the top. As proud partners of the Santos Tour Down Under, we celebrate Tristan’s journey from the streets of Norwood to riding among the world’s best.

item.title: Christian Prudhomme explains the decision to close the last 44 kilometers of stage 3 to the public
item.description: The director of @letourdefrance, Christian Prudhomme, explained the decision to close the last 44 kilometers of stage 3 to the public because of the fires in the Pyrénées-Orientales. He said access would be limited to riders and essential organization vehicles. He also said the priority is the affected populations and thanked the people of Les Angles, the finish town, for their understanding and civility. #TDF2026

item.title: Stage 3: Granollers to Les Angles, 195.9 km
item.description: Stage 3 of #TDF2026 runs from Ajuntament de Granollers to Les Angles mountain station. The stage is 195.9 km long, starts at 12:10 CEST and is expected to finish at 17:08 CEST. It includes one category 1 climb and three category 3 climbs, with the intermediate sprint at km 98.4.

item.title: Stage 3: Granollers to Les Angles, 195.9 km

item.title: Stage 3: Granollers to Les Angles, 195.9 km

item.title: The Tour crosses exceptional landscapes. Let’s help preserve them.
item.description: The Tour travels through some of the world’s most beautiful landscapes. Let’s help protect them. During periods of extreme heat, a single cigarette butt thrown into nature can be enough to start a wildfire. Please use a pocket ashtray, take your litter with you, and stay vigilant. Nine out of ten wildfires are caused by human activity, and more than half result from carelessness or dangerous behaviour. If you spot a fire, call the emergency services immediately on 18 or 112. Don’t let a moment of carelessness spoil the celebration. The Tour showcases our regions. Together, let’s protect them.

item.title: The Tour de France is the toughest race in the world
item.description: The Tour de France is the toughest race in the world. It has been a difficult start, but our morale remains high and we’re ready to keep fighting. We’re 100% behind Cian and we’ll fight with him until the very end. This race has only just begun. There are still three weeks to go, and the stages that suit him best are still to come. #RodamosJuntos | @movistar_es

item.title: Lorenzo saves Romain today
item.description: A bike change between Romain Grégoire and Lorenzo Germani just before the serious racing began. A top 10 in the Tour can sometimes come down to the smallest details. That’s what teammates are for.

item.description: Yannis Voisard, from the Jura region and riding for Tudor Pro Cycling Team, is the best Swiss rider in the Tour de France after this first weekend. Interview with Patrick Delétroz on Radio Télévision Suisse (RTS): https://www.rts.ch/…/la-rencontre-sport-le-cycliste-yannis-… #tdr #tdr2026 #tdrnonstop #tourdefrance

item.title: Stage 3 on Monday, July 6, 2026: stage confirmed, but no public or publicity caravan in France
item.description: Stage 3 on Monday, July 6, 2026 will go ahead, but there will be no spectators or publicity caravan on French territory. The public is asked not to go to the roadside or to the finish area. Due to the fire currently affecting the Pyrénées-Orientales and to allow emergency services to be fully mobilized, the 3rd stage of the Tour de France will take place without spectators and without the publicity caravan on French territory (Ur–Les Angles section). #TDF2026 — @LeTour Jul 5, 2026

item.title: Tour de France stage 3 to be held without spectators or publicity caravan in France
item.description: Important information for Monday, July 6, 2026: the third stage of the Tour de France will go ahead, but without spectators or the publicity caravan on French territory, on the Ur to Les Angles section. Because of the fire currently affecting the Pyrénées-Orientales and to allow emergency services to be fully mobilized, this exceptional decision was taken to limit the impact on the department, ensure everyone's safety, and allow firefighters, police, and all other services to remain fully focused on fighting the fires. Our thoughts are with the firefighters, security forces, emergency services, elected officials, staff, and volunteers working tirelessly. Their commitment is exemplary. We ask everyone to act responsibly: do not try to go to the Tour de France route, avoid any non-essential travel in the affected areas, follow the instructions of firefighters and police, do not overload emergency lines, and call 112 in case of emergency. Use only official channels for information and do not share unverified information. Today, the best way to support the Tour de France is to show solidarity and civic responsibility so that those protecting the region can carry out their mission in the best possible conditions.

item.title: Côte de Begue (category 2): Alex Molenaar takes maximum points
item.description: At 74 km, the Côte de Begue (category 2) climb was contested for the points: 1) Alex Molenaar, 5 pts 2) Felix Engelhardt, 3 pts 3) Frank van den Broek, 2 pts. Alex Molenaar moves into the provisional lead of the mountains classification and strengthens his claim on the polka-dot jersey. #TDF2026

item.title: Le Tour de France Posted
#TDF2026 biodiversity: Tarragone > Barcelone
item.description: #TDF2026 biodiversity: Tarragone > Barcelone. Created in 1983, the Ebre Delta Natural Park, located in southern Catalonia in the province of Tarragone, is one of the largest wetlands in the Mediterranean. Where the river meets the sea, more than 360 bird species find refuge, including the pink flamingo, which has become the park’s emblem.

item.title: From the Tour de France to Un si grand soleil
item.description: Christian Prudhomme, director of Le Tour de France, took part in the fiction series by playing himself in a scene filmed at Vivez Montpellier Méditerranée. His experience on set, his passion for cycling, and his view of Montpellier and the city's place in Tour history are featured in this special encounter between the Grande Boucle and Un si grand soleil.

item.title: The 2027 stages are officially here
item.description: The 2027 stages are officially here. Here is what is waiting for the peloton: Our longest race ever: 876 kilometres across the men’s race and 397 kilometres for the women. Henley Beach, Glenelg, and Christies Beach will host stage starts, while Victor Harbor is set for the finish. Stage 1 is usually for the sprinters, but this year the GC contenders have a chance to take an early advantage on THINK! Road Safety Race Day 1. Explore the stages in detail at the link in our bio. @santos_ltd #TourDownUnder

item.title: From the Tour de France to Un si grand soleil, it’s just a pedal stroke away
item.description: Christian Prudhomme, director of @letourdefrance, took part in the fiction series by playing himself in the show filmed in Montpellier. His experience on set, his passion for cycling, and his view of Montpellier and the city’s place in Tour history are featured in this unique meeting between the Tour de France and Un si grand soleil.

item.title: Tour de France in Catalunya, between Tarragona and Barcelona
item.description: Today, the Tour de France is in Catalunya, between Tarragona and Barcelona. After welcoming the champions, ride the EuroVelo 8 Mediterranean Route, which connects Andalucia to Cyprus for more than 7,000 km along the Mediterranean Sea. In this Catalan section, stunning views await. Photos: Pere Duran/Consorci de les Vies Verdes de Girona; Jase Wilson/Patronat de Turisme Costa Brava Pirineus de Girona; Pere Duran/Consorci de les Vies Verdes de Girona; Pere Duran/Consorci de les Vies Verdes de Girona; Pere Duran/Consorci de les Vies Verdes de Girona #TDF2026 #laveniravélo #eurovelo

item.title: Montréal awaits you
item.description: From September 20 to 27, the 2026 UCI Road World Championships will turn Montréal into a stage for free, spectacular, and accessible competition for all. With 1,000 athletes, 80 countries, and 13 events, now is the time to plan your trip. Book your visit today and experience one of the biggest sporting events of the year. 👉 https://www.montreal2026.org/en/plan-your-trip/

item.title: It was a serious crash, but we’re glad to learn that no rider sustained any major injury. See you tomorrow, guys! 💪 #TDF2026
item.description: It was a serious crash, but we’re glad to learn that no rider sustained any major injury. See you tomorrow, guys! 💪 #TDF2026 Video — XDS Astana Team (@XDSAstanaTeam) 🇪🇸 🇫🇷 Medical update: @LeTour No fractures or serious injuries for our riders, but @NicoVino13, @haroldtejada1 and @simovelasco suffered bruises and multiple extensive abrasions. #XDSAstanaTeam #TDF2026 — https://x.com/XDSAstanaTeam/status/2073465579298488395 — @LeTour Jul 4, 2026

item.title: As the Tour de France begins today, @lavuelta and @peaceandsport reflect on the power of sport beyond competition.
item.description: As the Tour de France begins today, @lavuelta and @peaceandsport reflect on the power of sport beyond competition. With Victor Langellotti, the first Monégasque WorldTour rider, we celebrate cycling as a force that connects people, builds dialogue, and brings communities together. Being a Champion for Peace is not only about winning—it’s about the impact we create along the way. #lavueltaesmás #BePartOfWhatMatters

item.title: Best wishes from the Tour de Romandie to Elise Chabbey
item.description: All the best from the Tour de Romandie to Elise Chabbey. The #TDRF2025 winner is expecting a child, which is why she will not ride the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift or any other race this season.

item.title: The chase for the yellow jersey begins at the 2026 Tour de France Grand Départ

item.title: Museeuw's first victory in de Ronde | Ronde van Vlaanderen 1993
With the Grand Départ of #TDF2026, discover Catalonia by gravel bike
item.description: With the Grand Départ of #TDF2026, discover Catalonia by gravel bike. Grand Tour Catalunya Gravel offers endless variety: 5 sections, 42 stages, 2,480 km, and 14,546 m of climbing. A tailor-made experience, at your own pace, with heritage and nature on every pedal stroke. From the Mediterranean coast to inland tracks, it reflects the spirit of the first three stages of the Tour: varied, demanding, and spectacular routes. For more information: https://grandtour.catalunya.com/…/pedalez-le-grand-tour-de…/ @catexperience #TDF2026 #GrandTourCatalunya #Catalogne

item.title: Interview: What it takes to organize a WorldTour race

item.title: Territorio Románico | Stage 4
item.description: Stage 4, called Territorio Románico, will highlight two districts in Burgos with extraordinary historical and artistic heritage: Merindad de Valdivielso and La Bureba. Both areas preserve some of the finest examples of rural Romanesque art in Castilla y León, set in beautiful natural landscapes and known for the quality of their monumental sculpture, architectural uniqueness, and excellent state of preservation. The stage will start in Palazuelos de Muñó and finish in Briviesca. Territorio Románico | Stage 4. Palazuelos de Muñó to Briviesca. Friday, August 7. 178 km. Sign-on starts at 11:10 a.m. More information about this stage: https://www.vueltaburgos.com/es/4_etapa/ Vuelta a Burgos, a whole province to discover. #VueltaBurgos #UCIProSeries #Burgos #ProvinciadeBurgos

The stage will start in Palazuelos de Muñó and finish in Briviesca. Territorio Románico | Stage 4. Palazuelos de Muñó to Briviesca. Friday, August 7. 178 km. item.title: Special surprise for supporters of #8Etapas1Meta
item.description: We have a very special surprise for everyone supporting the #8Etapas1Meta challenge. Thanks to @lavuelta, we have this official jersey, which will be one of the prizes in the giveaways we will hold during the challenge. Starting on July 6, Juanjo (@ciclista_de_pueblo) will ride across Spain from north to south in 8 stages, carrying a message that goes far beyond cycling. Our goals are to raise awareness of childhood cancer, raise funds for research, encourage more blood donations, and inspire new people to become bone marrow donors. This jersey is just one example of the huge support we are receiving. Thanks to La Vuelta for joining this initiative and helping this message reach even further. If you want to be part of the challenge, follow us, share this reel, and help #8Etapas1Meta reach as many people as possible. Because together we can pedal toward one goal: a future with more research, more solidarity, and more hope. 💛🚴 #8Etapas1Meta #CancerInfantil #Investigación

item.title: 18 new municipalities receive La Vuelta's 2026 Más Bici seal
item.description: 18 new municipalities receive La Vuelta's 2026 Más Bici seal. A total of 23 municipalities have now been recognised for their commitment to everyday bicycle use and more sustainable mobility. With this initiative, La Vuelta seeks to inspire institutions to encourage daily cycling and integrate it more and more into people's lives. Cycling is much more than sport. It is a way to move, to live and to care for our environment.

item.title: A night to remember in Barcelona as the Grand Départ of the Tour de France 2026 begins
item.description: Barcelona welcomed the 23 teams of the Grand Départ of the Tour de France 2026 with a ceremony that turned the city streets into a stage. From the Recinte Modernista de Sant Pau to the Sagrada Família, sport, music, heritage, and culture came together in a unique show that moved thousands of people and was seen around the world. The opening of a weekend that is already part of Barcelona and Tour history has begun.

item.title: A dedicated Barcelona kit with Santini Cycling Wear
item.description: We couldn't start from Barcelona without designing a dedicated kit with Santini Cycling Wear. Barcelona is too beautiful not to deserve its own Grand Départ kit. #TDF2026

item.title: VELUX, official bib number sponsor of the Tour de France 2026
item.description: Every rider has a number. What's yours? VELUX, the official bib number sponsor of the Tour de France 2026, is giving you the chance to make it personal and win exclusive prizes. Create your bib and enter the draw. Generate yours here: https://www.velux.co.uk/campaign/velux-on-tour… #ChasingDaylight #VELUXOnTour

item.title: The lanterne rouge on the Tour de France
item.description: On the Tour de France, the lanterne rouge is more than just a position in the race; it represents a mindset of determination, perseverance, and resilience in the face of every challenge. VELUX is the official partner of the race numbers for the Tour de France 2026. #VELUXonTour #ChasingDaylight

item.title: The MobiliTour returns for @letourdefrance
item.description: From July 2 to 26, the team is back on the road. Meet us at the Barcelona Fan Park from July 2 to 5, at the Bordeaux Fan Park on July 9 and 10, and at the departure animation areas throughout the Tour. Between stages, the team will link the stage towns by bike, train, and intermediate vehicle with @midipilemobility. At the stand, there will be fun workshops for all ages, projects promoting active and gentle mobility, and talks with the @mobilitour_ team and the @univertsport association. An adventure led by the @univertsport association, with support from @ademeofficiel, @letourdefrance, @midipilemobility, @active.giving, @pousse_papier_fr, @openrunner, @apirun1, and @ogarun_sport_outdoor. #TDF2026 #mobilitour #mobilités #bougerensemble

item.title: 11 Australians set to line up at @letourdefrance
item.description: 11 Australians are set to start cycling's biggest race, including Michael Storer, Ben O'Connor, and Jai Hindley. It will also be a Tour debut for Sebastian Berwick and a final Tour de France appearance for Luke Durbridge. #TDF26

item.title: Experience the Renewi Tour 2026 from the front row
item.description: Five days of WorldTour racing and five days of premium hospitality. Experience the Renewi Tour 2026 from the front row with VIP Arrivée, an exclusive VIP Départ in Blankenberge, or the ultimate race-day experience from inside the peloton in our VIP guest car. Enjoy top-level cycling, great food and drinks, and unforgettable moments along the route. Tickets and info on our website. #RenewiTour #GoWithGolazo

item.title: The contract is signed. The 6th stage of the 83rd Tour de Pologne will be a mountain stage
item.description: Together with BUKOVINA Resort, we are continuing our long-standing partnership. On 8 August 2026, the 6th stage of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour will be a true cycling celebration: a 126 km mountain stage with almost 3,000 m of climbing and the iconic BUKOVINA Wall in Gliczarów. The finish on the climb in Bukowina Tatrzańska could decide the outcome of the entire race. On the same day, more than 2,000 amateurs will start on the same route in the ORLEN Tour de Pologne Amateurs - Ryszard Szurkowski Memorial. A fan zone is planned at Rusiński Wierch, with activities and plenty of attractions for supporters.

item.title: Contract signed: the 6th stage of the 2026 Tour de Pologne will finish in Bukowina Tatrzańska
item.description: Contract signed! Together with @bukovinaresort, we are continuing our long-standing partnership. On 8 August 2026, the 6th stage of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour will be a true celebration of cycling: 126 km, nearly 3,000 m of climbing, and the iconic Ściana BUKOVINA in Gliczarów. The uphill finish in Bukowina Tatrzańska could decide the outcome of the entire race. On the same day, more than 2,000 amateur riders will start on the same route in the ORLEN Tour de Pologne Amatorów - Memoriał Ryszarda Szurkowskiego. The fan zone is planned for Rusiński Wierch.

item.title: Lucky 13 gets a new look for @letourdefrance
item.description: Lucky 13 just got a new look. The @redbullborahansgrohe special @letourdefrance kit is built around the number that has followed us through some of our biggest moments. An upside-down 13 marks our 13th Tour de France, with bold colour blocking from bike to kit, white shoulders fading into deep blue, and clean transparent layers. A cycling superstition turned into a full Tour design. Ready for our 13th Tour de France. See you at the Grand Départ 🇫🇷🤩 __________ 📷 @friesoooooo

item.title: Willunga Hill returns for 2027
item.description: Willunga Hill is back for 2027. This storied climb will feature four peloton passes on Race Day 5, two for the men and two for the women, before a decisive uphill finish that rewards explosive power and stamina. That means double the roadside action on what promises to be an iconic day of racing. Explore the 2027 stages via the link in our bio. @santos_ltd #TourDownUnder @uci_cycling @uci_wwt

item.title: Willunga Hill returns for Race Day 5 at the 2027 Santos Tour Down Under
item.description: Willunga Hill is back for 2027. Race Day 5 will feature four peloton passes on the climb, two for the men and two for the women, before a decisive uphill finish. It promises a day of intense racing and plenty of roadside action.

item.title: Inside My Race brings a new way to follow the Tour de France
item.description: Every stage of the Tour de France tells a story, and now fans can see more of it. In partnership with the Tour de France and Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift, a new generative AI-powered fan experience called Inside My Race combines route information, rider rankings, expert analysis, and race history to show the tactics and key moments as each stage unfolds.

item.title: Inside My Race offers a new way to follow the Tour de France
item.description: Every stage of the Tour de France tells a story, and Inside My Race gives fans a continuous, tailored view of the race as it unfolds. Created with Capgemini and the Tour de France, it combines route information, rider rankings, expert analysis, and race history for both new and experienced fans.

item.title: Ride The Route: Lloyds Tour of Britain 2026
item.description: Your routes are here. Ride specially selected sections of Stage 2 of the women’s race and Stage 4 of the men’s race, just days before the pros. Whether you are chasing a new challenge or soaking up the atmosphere, this is your chance to be part of the Lloyds Tour of Britain. Pick your route and get ready to ride. What is included: instant results, exceptional rider support, free event photos, and more. Ticket sales open this Friday.

item.title: Ride The Route: Lloyds Tour of Britain 2026
item.description: Your routes are here. Ride specially selected sections of Stage 2 of the women’s race and Stage 4 of the men’s race, just days before the pros. What is included: instant results, exceptional rider support, free event photos, and more. Ticket sales open this Friday.

item.title: Lloyds Tour of Britain Ride The Route 2026
item.description: Following the success of the inaugural Lloyds Tour of Britain Ride The Route, the event returns for 2026 with two events, one for the men’s race and one for the women’s race. Riders can test themselves against the route just days before the World Tour pro pelotons. Visit the website to check the routes and pre-register now. Entries open at 10am on Friday. #TourofBritain2026 #TourofBritainRideTheRoute

item.title: #TDF2026 marks an important anniversary for inclusion of blind and visually impaired fans
item.description: #TDF2026 marks an important anniversary for inclusion of blind and visually impaired fans. For 30 years, the Tour de France road book has been adapted into Braille, then enlarged print, and distributed free of charge by HandiCaPZéro. This anniversary is a special opportunity to highlight tandem cycling for blind people. On 26 July on the Champs-Elysées, discover 10 tandems made up of a sighted pilot and a blind or visually impaired co-pilot who will ride down the Champs-Elysées and cover several kilometres to introduce the public to this inclusive discipline. #LAveniràVélo

item.title: Save this post for later: 2027 is shaping up to be big
item.description: Save this post for later. 2027 is shaping up to be big. Don’t forget: 10:40am – Men’s race start; 12:10pm – Women’s race start. Over the final three days, the men and women will race on the same course, on the same day, and over the same distance, becoming the first race anywhere to deliver this new format. That means you can find a vantage point along the route and watch both WorldTour pelotons pass on the same day. Explore the stages at the link in our bio. @santos_ltd #TourDownUnder

item.title: The Tour de France starts on Saturday from Barcelona
item.description: On Saturday, the Tour de France starts from Barcelona for three weeks of racing, crossing the Pyrenees, the Massif Central, the Alps and reaching Paris after almost 3,500 km. But cycling across Europe is not only for champions: with EuroVelo, the European cycle route network managed by the European Cyclists’ Federation (ECF), you can travel by bicycle along 17 different routes, more than 90,000 km and 39 countries. Photos: P.Verticale; Jase Wilson/Patronat de Turisme Costa Brava Pirineus de Girona; Pere Duran/Consorci de les Vies Verdes de Girona #TDF2026 #laveniravélo #eurovelo

item.title: The 2026 Tour de France with Banane de Guadeloupe & Martinique
item.description: The 2026 Tour de France is in full swing with Banane de Guadeloupe & Martinique. Official Tour supplier for the past seven years, the French banana returns to brighten the race with natural energy and a smile. This year again, every click counts: visit the website to generate a donation of French bananas for Restos du Cœur as part of the #KM97 operation. Goal: beat all generosity records and offer as many bananas as possible to the association. Final result at Saint-Symphorien, at the 97th kilometer of stage 7.

item.title: Rexel France continues its journey alongside the Tour de France for a fourth year
item.description: For its fourth year alongside the Tour de France, Rexel France continues its journey: supporting the electrification of regions. On the Tour, energy makes the difference. On the ground too. Across France, projects are accelerating the energy transition: solar power, electric mobility, energy efficiency, and more. Rexel, a distributor of electrification solutions, is there to support them. During #TDF2025, discover “Vous êtes au courant,” a series focused on the projects and practical solutions transforming local areas.

item.title: Santos Tour Down Under 2027 stage routes announced
item.description: The stages are set. In 2027, the Santos Tour Down Under will be the longest and toughest on record. The men’s race features six scenic stages with classic challenges and new terrain. Climbers will be pushed and sprinters will be tested. The women’s race includes three major stages with new climbs, varied start and finish locations, and routes that demand serious stamina. Explore the stages.

item.title: Congratulations to the new Polish champions
item.description: Congratulations to the new Polish champions! A thrilling weekend of the Polish Road Championships took place on the roads of the Warmian-Masurian Voivodeship. In the women’s competition, Dominika Włodarczyk of UAE Team ADQ was unbeatable, winning two elite Polish titles by taking both the individual time trial and the road race. In the men’s time trial, Michał Kwiatkowski regained the Polish champion’s jersey after three years. The road race brought a big surprise, as 23-year-old Kacper Maciejuk claimed the first national title of his career and will race in the white-and-red jersey with the eagle on his chest for the next year. Congratulations to all the medallists and good luck for the rest of the season. See you soon on the roads of the 4th Tour de Pologne Women UCI ProSeries and the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour! 🇵🇱👏 📷 @stuchlikphoto @grupaorlen #ORLEN

In the men’s time trial, Michał Kwiatkowski regained the Polish champion’s jersey after three years. The road race brought a big surprise, as 23-year-old Kacper Maciejuk claimed the first national title of his career and will race in the white-and-red jersey with the eagle on his chest for the next year. item.description: La Vuelta does not want a 100% predictable race where technology tells you in advance who will win. The debate about watts and race radios is no coincidence; @lavuelta is looking for unpredictability so the spectacle does not fade. #club343 #sportbusiness

item.title: Five days, two races, countless emotions at the Tour de Suisse 2026
item.description: Five days, two races, countless emotions. The Tour de Suisse 2026 had it all: in both the women’s and men’s races, there were spectacular breakaways, thrilling mountain finishes, and finales that stayed open until the end. Thank you to everyone in the peloton, to the teams, to all the helpers and partners, and especially to everyone cheering from the roadside. You make this race what it is. See you in 2027! Montage @herr_roja #tourdesuisse #tds2026

item.title: Stage 5: Caleruega to Lagunas de Neila (E)
item.description: 🏔✨ “From earth to sky” | Stage 5 🚩 Caleruega 🏁 Lagunas de Neila (E) 🗓 Saturday, August 8 📏 137 km ✍️ Sign-on from 11:51 a.m. ⏰ 1:14 p.m. > 4:45 p.m. ℹ️ More information about this stage👇 vueltaburgos.com/es/5_etapa/ #VueltaBurgos #Burgos #ProvinciadeBurgos

item.title: Stage 5: Caleruega to Lagunas de Neila
item.description: 🏔✨ Under the slogan “From earth to sky,” the fifth and final stage of 137 km will start in Caleruega and finish at the iconic Lagunas de Neila. It will be held on Saturday, August 8, during the celebration of Santo Domingo de Guzmán, patron saint of the province of Burgos. He is also the patron saint of astronomers, which is especially relevant this year because of the eclipse on August 12 🌑🌝 🏔✨ “From earth to sky” | Stage 5 🚩 Caleruega 🏁 Lagunas de Neila (E) 🗓 Saturday, August 8 📏 137 km ✍️ Sign-on from 11:51 a.m. ⏰ 1:14 p.m. > 4:45 p.m. ℹ️ More information about this stage👇 https://www.vueltaburgos.com/es/5_etapa/ 😍 Vuelta a #Burgos, a whole province to discover 💜 #VueltaBurgos #UCIProSeries #Burgos #ProvinciadeBurgos

item.title: New episode of the Official La Vuelta Podcast
item.description: This week’s episode focuses on one of the biggest names in La Vuelta history: Primož Roglič. The Slovenian has the chance to win his fifth La Vuelta and become the most successful rider in the race’s history. Could 2026 be the year he makes history? Listen to the episode with @albermg.

item.description: Request the #TDR newsletter and find out who will be the lucky 1,000th subscriber after it is sent to subscribers this Thursday. The newsletter includes news, images, and behind-the-scenes content, including Tadej Pogačar of UAE Team Emirates - XRG, who recently achieved a double at the Tour de Suisse. The 1,000th fan to subscribe in the coming hours will receive two VIP tickets for the #TDR2027, for a stage of their choice. item.title: Tour de Pologne Junior registration opens on July 1 at 10:00
item.description: Registration for this year’s Tour de Pologne Junior - Memoriał Mariana Więckowskiego opens on July 1 at 10:00. Young riders will once again have the chance to race on the same roads that the professionals will use later in the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour. This year’s edition will take place in three cities: Koszalin on 3 August, Szczecin on 4 August, and Zielona Góra on 5 August. A new registration format applies this year, with sign-up available only through the website. A signed parental consent form must be attached to each entry; for club entries, all consents must be submitted in one PDF file. More information is available at tourdepolognejunior.pl. #ORLEN

item.title: La Vuelta is not about sterrato or cobbles. Its terrain is the impossible climb
item.description: La Vuelta does not look for sterrato like the Giro or cobbles like the Tour. Its terrain is the impossible climb. A finish of one or two kilometres of pure gradient can tell a more epic story than any sprint, and that is @lavuelta in its purest form 🔥 #club343 #sportbusiness

item.title: Four stage wins in five days, plus the overall classification - our time in Switzerland was a joy! 🇨🇭
item.description: Four stage wins in five days, plus the overall classification - our time in Switzerland was a joy! 🇨🇭 A few days after the @tourdesuisse_official, you’re invited behind the scenes with @tadejpogacar and the team in our latest YouTube special. Out now, our BTS video from Switzerland gives an inside look at how Tadej claimed another memorable WorldTour stage race victory 🍿🎥 And that’s not to forget a brilliant win from the 🦎 #WeAreUAE | #TourdeSuisse 🔗 Click the link in our bio for the full video!

item.title: The Giro d’Italia is more than a race
item.description: The Giro d’Italia is more than a sporting competition: it is a driver of economic growth and territorial promotion. The event generates a total impact of 2.1 billion euros, including 700 million in direct spending and 1.4 billion in indirect effects. It also has a significant social impact, estimated at 79 million euros, with a multiplier of 2.8. When the race starts outside Italy, it also becomes a tool of cultural diplomacy. After the start in Tirana, this year it was Bulgaria's turn. The Giro is a true infrastructure that creates economic, social, and cultural value for the country.

item.title: Two days at the Tour de Suisse
item.description: Two days at the Tour de Suisse. A lot of heat, a lot of cameras, and a behind-the-scenes look like we’ve never had before. As cyclists ourselves, we thought we had a pretty good idea of how hard this sport is. But watching the pros race through 35-degree heat and one of the biggest storms Switzerland has seen in a long time, stage after stage, was something else. The speed. The suffering. The focus. All while making it look almost easy. This sport is brutal and beautiful. Grateful we got to witness it up close. Thank you, Tour de Suisse. @tourdesuisse_official #tourdesuisse #cyclingphotography #tadejpogacar #cycling

item.title: AG Insurance - Soudal wins the team classification at the Tour de Suisse Women 2026
item.description: Sometimes a race moves so fast that the celebration has to wait. 🏆 That is exactly what happened to @aginaginsurancesoudal: they more than earned their win in the team classification at the #TourdeSuisseWomen 2026, but by the time the result was confirmed, the riders were already heading to the bus. So let's make up for it now. Huge congratulations on the best team performance of this Tour de Suisse Women. All week long you showed strength, consistency and real team spirit. 👏 The celebration wasn't missed though: the alcohol-free Prosecco by @jpchenet.official was already on its way. Bravo, AG Insurance – Soudal! 🥂

item.title: New course for the Grand Prix Cycliste de Québec
item.description: This year, the Grand Prix Cycliste de Québec route has been slightly modified. The new, slightly shorter circuit allows for more laps. 10.3 km per lap, 20 laps, total distance 206 km. Start and finish on the Plains of Abraham. Riders pass you up to 20 times. See you on 11 September 2026 in Québec.

item.title: Grand Prix Cycliste de Montréal will use the UCI Road World Championships circuit
item.description: This year, the Grand Prix Cycliste de Montréal will use the Mont-Royal circuit from the UCI Road World Championships Montréal 2026. 13.4 km per lap, 16 laps, total distance 214.4 km, around Mont-Royal. For the first time, riders will take Saint-Urbain Street before climbing back up Avenue du Parc. See you on 13 September 2026 in Montréal.

item.title: Contest: win the signed yellow jersey
item.description: Contest: win the signed Maillot Jaune from the winner @isaac_deltoro_romero1 of the 2026 @criteriumdudauphine. To enter: follow @maillotjaunelcl and @criteriumdudauphine; mention as many cycling friends as possible in the comments; share this post to your story and tag us, or repost it on your account with the share option. The more cycling fans you tag, the better your chances of winning. Contest ends on July 4, 2026, at the start of the Tour. Good luck! #JeuConcours #MaillotJaune #Cyclisme #isaacdeltoro

item.title: The Tour de Suisse is lived everywhere
item.description: The Tour de Suisse is not only on the road. It is lived everywhere passion burns: by the roadside, cheering in the fan zone, or wherever you follow the race live. This year we were official partners of the Tour de Suisse for the first time. We equipped the volunteers and also went all out in the fan zone. From our 400 m bike challenge to discovering the high-end @van_rysel bikes used by the @decathloncmacgmteam, and chatting with our team, there was something for every cycling fan. A huge thank you to @tourdesuisse_official for all the эмоtions. And you? How did you cheer for the race? Tell us in the comments. #TDS #TourDeSuisse #TDS2026 #VanRysel #DecathlonSwitzerland

item.title: Hat-trick for Marlen Reusser at the Tour de Suisse
item.description: Hat-tricks aren’t just for the football World Cup. @marlenreusser won her third @tourdesuisse_official yesterday on home soil. 2023 - 2025 - 2026 #UCIWWT #TourDeSuisse

item.title: Volta Catalunya at the BISE presentation event
item.description: As a founding member of BISE, Volta was present at the presentation of the alliance between the eight major international sporting events in Barcelona and Catalonia. The goal is to take a step forward in consolidating the sporting leadership of the city and the region.

item.title: Remembering Sergio Corral
item.description: Today we lost Sergio Corral, a colleague and friend of IDJ for many years. His voice accompanied us at events including the Vuelta a Burgos. Always with a smile. Our thoughts are with his family and loved ones. Rest in peace.

item.title: Vuelta a Burgos mourns the loss of Sergio Corral
item.description: The Vuelta a Burgos deeply regrets the passing of Sergio Corral, a much-loved person who accompanied us for many years. Sergio brought voice, warmth, and professionalism to many shared moments. Always by our side, always with a smile, he leaves an unforgettable memory among those of us who were lucky enough to work with him and enjoy his company. Our deepest condolences to his family, friends, and loved ones. Thank you for everything, Sergio. Rest in peace.

item.title: Vuelta a Burgos mourns the death of Sergio Corral
item.description: The Vuelta a Burgos deeply regrets the death of Sergio Corral, a much-loved person who accompanied us for many years. item.title: Tour de Pologne stage 1 starts in Pomeranian Voivodeship on August 3
item.description: On August 3, Pomeranian Voivodeship will become the center of the cycling world, as stage 1 of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour begins there. The peloton will start from Skwer Kościuszki in Gdynia and finish in Koszalin. The race will be broadcast in more than 65 countries. The post also highlights places to visit in the region, including Bory Tucholskie, Kashubia, the Vistula Spit, Malbork Castle, and Hel.

item.title: Reusser: “The best scenario I could have imagined”
item.description: After winning the Tour de Suisse overall, her third title, Marlen Reusser says she feels relieved, proud, and very satisfied. Despite injuries from her crash at the Tour of Flanders and a very difficult Giro d’Italia, the 34-year-old from Bern triumphed in her home stage race. A withdrawal from the Tour de Suisse was never really considered: “It is something so emotional. We told ourselves: I will definitely do the Tour de Suisse. That it worked out like this is a huge joy.”

item.title: How to descend and corner made simple with @tourdesuisse_official winner and time trial world champion Marlen Reusser
item.description: How to descend and corner made simple with @tourdesuisse_official winner and time trial world champion Marlen Reusser. This week, Marlen showed once again why she is the best time trialist in the world after taking the yellow jersey on Stage 4. In this interview, the queen of fast corners reminds us that good technique often feels counterintuitive: don’t brake in the corner, don’t lean your body into the corner, lean the bike instead, and push the bike into the ground.

item.title: The whole world is watching the women, and we love it 🔥 Tour de Suisse Stage 5
item.description: The whole world is watching the women, and we love it 🔥 Tour de Suisse Stage 5, featuring steep climbs and a massive crowd! 🫶🏼 That’s a wrap. These past 5 days were out of this world. Thanks to everyone who was part of it 🩷 #girlswhoride #sharetheroad #tourdesuisse #mountainpasses

item.title: Riccitello on the rise 🆙 Matthew Riccitello took 5th place in the mountain showdown of the Tour de Suisse 2026 to finish seventh overall
item.description: Matthew Riccitello took 5th place in the mountain showdown of the Tour de Suisse 2026 to finish seventh overall. The young American paced himself well as Tadej Pogačar launched his move to victory, completing a solid week in warm conditions. Matthew Riccitello from 8.9 km to 5.3 km to go: Time 10:49, average speed 20.0 km/h, max speed 25.7 km/h, average power 340 W, max power 600 W, VAM 1801 m/h. In comparison, Tadej Pogačar produced the following over the same 3.6 km sector: Time 9:39, average speed 22.6 km/h, max speed 29.6 km/h, average power 470 W, max power 820 W, VAM 2029 m/h. 📸 Getty Images

item.title: An outstanding performance from @lennymrtz on the final stage of the @tourdesuisse_official
item.description: An outstanding performance from @lennymrtz on the final stage of the @tourdesuisse_official. After spending the day in the breakaway and taking the HC Col de la Croix KOM sprint, where he won the @rideforgino prize, Lenny attacked on the final climb and looked set for a memorable solo victory. He fought all the way to the line, only to be overtaken by Pogačar inside the final kilometre, finishing just 7 seconds behind. A brave ride to be proud of! 👏👏👏 #RideAsOne #rideforGino #TourdeSuisse 📸 @sprintcycling

item.title: Tour de Suisse 2026 – the jerseys have been decided! Five days, five stages, and a fitting finish in Villars-sur-Ollon
item.description: Tour de Suisse 2026 – the jerseys have been decided! Five days, five stages, and a fitting finish in Villars-sur-Ollon. Here are the winners of the classifications: 💛 Overall classification – presented by Primeo Energie Tadej Pogačar caps an outstanding week and takes the overall victory. 💙 Points classification – presented by Lidl Schweiz Suisse Svizzera Consistency across all stages is rewarded with the points jersey. ⛰️ Mountains classification – presented by @europcar Louis Vervaeke collects mountain points and secures the mountains jersey. 🤍 Young rider classification – presented by LEND Mathias Vacek comes out on top among the young riders. Thank you to all the fans on the roadside, the volunteers, and our partners. See you in 2027! 🚴‍♂️ #TourdeSuisse #TdS2026 #Cycling

Here are the winners of the classifications: 💛 Overall classification – presented by @primeo_energie Tadej Pogačar caps an outstanding week and takes the overall victory. 💙 Points classification – presented by @lidlch Consistency across all stages is rewarded with the points jersey. ⛰️ Mountains classification – presented by @europcar Louis Vervaeke collects mountain points and secures the mountains jersey. 🤍 Young rider classification – presented by @lend_ch Mathias Vacek comes out on top among the young riders. Thank you to all the fans on the roadside, the volunteers, and our partners. See you in 2027! 🚴‍♂️ #TourdeSuisse #TdS2026 #Cycling

item.title: Powerful Pogačar puts the icing on the cake
item.description: Tadej Pogačar (UAE Team Emirates XRG) won the 2026 Tour de Suisse by a clear margin. The Slovenian once again came out on top on the queen stage around Villars-sur-Ollon, celebrating his third stage win of the race. On the 150.7-kilometre stage with 4,226 metres of climbing, Pogačar beat Lenny Martinez (Bahrain Victorious) and […]

item.title: The new Tour de Suisse: A positive assessment after the premiere
item.description: The 89th edition of the Tour de Suisse featured major changes. For the first time anywhere in the world, an international stage race at the highest level was held with women’s and men’s races on the same day. The organisers draw a positive conclusion about the new concept and look back on the event.

item.title: Bravo Marlen! The Bernese rider has added a third Tour de Suisse title to her palmarès, with yesterday’s time trial victory to go with it.
item.description: Bravo Marlen! The Bernese rider has added a third Tour de Suisse title to her palmarès, with yesterday’s time trial victory to go with it. Tour de Romandie congratulates Marlen Reusser, who would also have been a favourite at #TDRF2026, which had to be postponed. See you in 2027! 💛🏆👍 #TDRF #romandie #tdrinside #tdrnonstop #tourdesuisse

item.title: Reusser secures overall victory with emphatic stage win
item.description: Marlen Reusser (Movistar Team) won the 2026 Tour de Suisse Women. On the demanding queen stage around Villars-sur-Ollon, the Swiss rider defended her yellow jersey and also won the final stage. After 3:07:20 of racing, Reusser finished ahead of Cédrine Kerbaol (EF Education Oatly) and Kasia Niewiadoma-Phinney (Canyon//SRAM).

item.title: Behind the Scenes: How is great cycling content created at the Tour de Suisse?
item.description: Behind the Scenes: How is great cycling content created at the Tour de Suisse? The social media team @soph_schall, @roadbike.andy, and @santina.malacarne talk about their experiences at the Tour de Suisse. They share how they create content that works, from spontaneous moments to detailed post-production. Sophia explains what she looks for in special moments: emotion, excitement, and the beauty of cycling. Santina reveals the secret to good content: authenticity and passion instead of copy-paste.

item.title: 🎤 Pascal Richard looks back on a memorable moment in his career during Friday's Étape Gourmande on June 19 at the Fairmont Le Montreux Palace...
item.description: 🎤 Pascal Richard looks back on a memorable moment in his career during Friday's Étape Gourmande on June 19 at the Fairmont Le Montreux Palace. On the occasion of the 30th anniversary of his Olympic title in Atlanta, the two-time Tour de Romandie winner and cyclo-cross world champion shares his memories and adds a nod to Tadej Pogačar. #tdr #TDR2026 #etapegourmande #pascalrichard #tdrnonstop #tdrinside #tourderomandie

item.title: 🎤 @Richard Chassot at the microphone for Étape Gourmande 2026. He talks about the importance of these events, the contribution of the Club Maillot Jaune, the Balade Gourmande, and the memorable image of the 2026 Tour de Romandie.

item.title: Stage 5 in Villars-sur-Ollon: the queen stage
item.description: Stage 5 in Villars-sur-Ollon is the queen stage. It goes over the Col de la Croix, features more than 4,000 metres of climbing, and ends with a finale that will decide the race. This is where Tour de Suisse 2026 is won, in the mountains. 📍 Villars-sur-Ollon #TourdeSuisse #TdS2026 #VillarssurOllon

item.title: Time trial spectacle in Aarburg!
item.description: Around Aarburg, the atmosphere was electric, with thousands of fans lining the course and cheering the riders on. It was a true celebration of cycling in a stunning setting. The Swiss highlight came from Marlen Reusser, who stormed to the stage win and took the yellow jersey. In the men's race, Tadej Pogačar was untouchable and extended his overall lead once again. What a day in Aarburg.

item.title: Happy birthday, Fabian!
item.description: From the race route to team briefings and the finish line, Fabian Wegmann always has the right eye for our #radklassiker. As Sports Director, he helps make Eschborn-Frankfurt what we love about cycling every year. Dear Fabian, we wish you a very happy birthday, strong legs for the year ahead, and plenty of tailwind. Send Fabian your birthday wishes in the comments.

item.description: Montserrat accompanied the start of the stage, what a luxury! Simply stunning. #VoltaCatalunya

item.title: Aarburg, time trial, two world champion jerseys on top.
item.description: In Aarburg, the time trial produced two world champion jerseys at the top. In the women's race, Marlen Reusser made a statement in the rainbow jersey, covering 23.8 km in 29:36, 11 seconds faster than Zoe Bäckstedt, and moving back into the yellow jersey. In the men's race, Tadej Pogačar beat Mathieu van der Poel by the narrowest of margins, measured to the hundredth of a second by TISSOT. A time trial could not be decided any closer. Tomorrow's queen stage is in Villars-sur-Ollon, where everything will be decided.

item.title: Pogačar wins split-second thriller in Aarburg
item.description: Tadej Pogačar (UAE Team Emirates XRG) edged out Mathieu van der Poel (Alpecin-Premier Tech) by the narrowest of margins in the 23.8-kilometre individual time trial around Aarburg to win stage 4. Just 0.04 seconds separated the pair at the finish. Tobias Foss (Netcompany INEOS) took third, 6 seconds down. On the fast course, several riders […]

item.title: Four hundredths. Stage 4 was rarely this close
item.description: Four hundredths of a second. Stage 4 was rarely this close. In the 23-kilometre individual time trial from Aarburg, Tadej Pogačar beat Mathieu van der Poel — the @tissot_official timing needed the final decimal place to decide the winner. A time trial could hardly be faster, more technical, or closer. --- Four hundredths of a second. The Aarburg time trial has never been this close. Over 23 kilometres against the clock, Tadej Pogačar edged out Mathieu van der Poel — and it took @tissot_official timing down to the final decimal to call it. A race of truth, settled by the smallest possible margin. 🇨🇭 ⏱️ #tourdesuisse #tds2026 #aarburg #timetrial #tissot procycling

item.description: At the Primeo Kids Race in Aarburg, the youngest riders raced their own time trial today. The clock was ticking, the little legs were spinning, and the speed was impressive. item.title: Copenhagen Sprint could not have happened without volunteers
item.description: We could not have held Copenhagen Sprint without volunteers. We are very grateful to everyone who showed up and contributed to the race. The first two photos are from a small celebration for the volunteers on Thursday, with prize draws and a chance to hear about their experiences at their posts. Thank you again — we hope to see many of you again next year.

item.title: Reusser wins the time trial and takes the overall lead
item.description: Marlen Reusser (Movistar Team) lived up to her status as the ITT favourite on stage 4 of the Tour de Suisse Women, moving to the top of the general classification. The rider from Bern won the 23.7-kilometre circuit around Aarburg in 29:36. Zoe Bäckstedt (Canyon//SRAM Zondacrypto) finished second, 11 seconds behind.

item.title: The grand start of the 3rd edition of the Volta Ciclista a Catalunya took place this morning in Santa Susanna
item.description: The grand start of the 3rd edition of the Volta Ciclista a Catalunya took place this morning in Santa Susanna. The town in the Maresme, which has the Sports Tourism Destination label thanks, among other things, to its high-level facilities and adapted accommodation, is a perfect destination for cycle touring or triathlon. In this setting, we enjoyed a first stage with start and finish in Santa Susanna, taking place through the Maresme and Vallès Oriental regions, which @odettefontanes and @pauquintanav experienced from the inside. This is only a taste of the many routes you can do from here to Montseny Natural Park and the Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park. The province of Barcelona has more than 3,000 km of routes and many mountain passes, such as: • Santuari de Queralt • Coll de Pal • Coll de Pradell • Coll de Fumanya • Collformic • Turó de l'Home • Coll de Bracons • Coll d'Estenalles A spectacular area with many options for cycle touring. You can find all the options on @bcnmoltmes' website.

item.title: A look back in pictures at the Étape Gourmande 2026.
item.description: A look back in pictures at the Étape Gourmande 2026. Refined dishes, a great atmosphere, memorable speeches, an engaging game, and above all unforgettable moments of sharing. This edition was also marked by the presence of many guests, including Valérie Dittli and Pascal Richard, invited on the occasion of the 30th anniversary of his Olympic title won in Atlanta. Thank you all for your presence and energy, which helped make this edition a real success. 🚴♂️🍽️✨ #tdr #tdrnostop #tdrinside #étapegourmande #FairmontMontreuxPalace #PascalRichard

item.title: Tour de Suisse 2026 – Stage 3 Highlights | Bad Ragaz
Narváez wins sprint duel after long breakaway
item.description: Jhonatan Narváez (UAE Team Emirates XRG) won stage 3 of the 2026 Tour de Suisse. The Ecuadorian came out on top in the sprint on the 157.9-kilometre circuit around Bad Ragaz, beating Xandro Meurisse (Pinarello – Q36.5 Pro Cycling Team). Magnus Cort (Uno-X Mobility) won the bunch sprint for third place. The stage began […]

item.title: Cofidis remains Partner of the Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour
item.description: We are pleased to announce that the French company Cofidis is continuing its long-standing cooperation with Lang Team and remains a Partner of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour. The brand has supported world cycling for years, sponsoring professional teams and partnering with some of the biggest races in the world. This year’s Tour de Pologne will take place under the slogan “North–South” and, from 3 to 9 August, will connect the Baltic Sea with the Tatra Mountains. A week of sporting excitement awaits, featuring the biggest stars of the world peloton.

item.description: We are pleased to announce that the French company Cofidis is continuing its long-standing cooperation with Lang Team and remains a Partner of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour. The brand has supported world cycling for years, sponsoring professional teams and partnering with some of the biggest races in the world. This year’s Tour de Pologne will take place under the slogan “North–South” and, from 3 to 9 August, will connect the Baltic Sea with the Tatra Mountains. A week of sporting excitement awaits, featuring the biggest stars of the world peloton. More information about the cooperation with COFIDIS and the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour can be found on our website. https://www.tourdepologne.pl/…/cofidis-pozostaje-partnerem…/

item.title: Bäckstedt wins stage 3 in Bad Ragaz
item.description: Zoe Bäckstedt (Canyon//SRAM Zondacrypto) won stage 3 of the Tour de Suisse Women. The Briton came out on top in the sprint on the 120.8-kilometre circuit around Bad Ragaz. Lily Williams (Human Powered Health) was second, with Shari Bossuyt (AG Insurance Soudal Team) completing the podium. Jasmin Liechti (Swiss Cycling) was the best Swiss rider in 5th place.

item.title: Tour de Romandie: Étape Gourmande 2026 in Montreux
item.description: The Tour de Romandie takes a different turn with Étape Gourmande 2026. Around 400 guests gathered on Friday lunchtime at the Fairmont Le Montreux Palace for an event focused on cycling, gastronomy, and sharing. The edition was attended by several personalities, including Valérie Dittli and Pascal Richard, invited on the occasion of the 30th anniversary of his Olympic title won in Atlanta.

Grégoire takes stage victory
item.description: Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United) won stage 2 of the 2026 Tour de Suisse. The Frenchman prevailed in a sprint from a breakaway group on the 157.7-kilometre circuit around Locarno. Marcel Camprubí (Pinarello – Q36.5 Pro Cycling Team) finished second, while Bart Lemmen (Team Visma | Lease a Bike) completed the podium.

item.title: Tour de Suisse 2026 – Stage 1 Highlights | Sondrio (IT) #srfsport
Pogačar wins opening stage in Sondrio
item.description: Tadej Pogačar (UAE Team Emirates XRG) won Stage 1 of the 2026 Tour de Suisse. The Slovenian attacked solo 70 kilometres from the finish on the 144-kilometre circuit around Sondrio and rode alone to victory on the opening day. Richard Carapaz (EF Education–EasyPost) finished second at 2:14, with Andrea Bagioli (Lidl-Trek) third.

item.title: Now open to everyone: ADAC Velotour 2027
item.description: Registration for the ADAC Velotour 2027 is now open to everyone. On May 1, 2027, your cycling highlight awaits as part of Eschborn-Frankfurt – The Classic. Closed roads, great atmosphere, and a day on a course that feels like a pro race. Demand is already high, as pre-registered and loyal participants had early access. In 2026, the ADAC Velotour sold out months before the event. Places are limited, so register early to secure the best available price.

item.title: De Vries wins the opener in Sondrio
item.description: Femke De Vries won stage 1 of the 2026 Tour de Suisse Women. The Dutch rider from Team Visma | Lease a Bike won a sprint against Ailish Lauren Dickson (FDJ United Suez) on the demanding circuit in Sondrio to claim her first professional victory. Cedrine Kerbaol (EF Education Oatly) finished third.

J-4: More than 400 guests expected on Friday at the Fairmont Le Montreux Palace for the Tour de Romandie Gourmet Stage
item.description: J-4! More than 400 guests are expected on Friday at the Fairmont Le Montreux Palace for the Tour de Romandie Gourmet Stage. Official Tour de Romandie 2026 aftermovie on the big screen. A look back at an exceptional edition dominated by Tadej Pogačar, with a Kahoot quiz between the tables. The evening will also celebrate the 30th anniversary of Pascal Richard's Olympic title in Atlanta, with him present. Double winner of the Tour de Romandie, he was also recently honored with a commemorative stele recognizing his exceptional career. Last year, he met Mauro Gianetti at the 2025 Gourmet Stage. A night full of emotion is expected on Friday. #TourDeRomandie #EtapeGourmande #PascalRichard #Atlanta1996 #TDR2026 TadejPogacar Montreux Cyclisme

item.title: J-4 for the Tour de Romandie Gourmet Stage
item.description: J-4 for the Tour de Romandie Gourmet Stage. This Friday, 19 June, more than 400 guests will gather at the Fairmont Le Montreux Palace to celebrate a 2026 Tour de Romandie edition that will be remembered. The official aftermovie will be shown on the big screen. The week was marked by Tadej Pogačar's dominance. An interactive Kahoot quiz between the tables is also planned. In the presence of Pascal Richard, the evening will celebrate the 30th anniversary of his 1996 Atlanta Olympic title. A double winner of the Tour de Romandie and a regular at the event, where he met Mauro Gianetti last year, he has recently been honored with the inauguration of a commemorative stele recalling his Olympic title, his contribution to Swiss cycling, and his friendship with the Ciclo club Spongano in Puglia. The evening promises an exceptional mix of passion, memories, and fine food. #TourDeRomandie #EtapeGourmande #PascalRichard #Atlanta1996 #TadejPogacar #Montreux #FairmontMontreuxPalace #tdrnonstop #romandie #TDR2026 #tdrinside

item.title: Barcelona will crown the #VoltaCatalunya champions again
item.description: A cycling capital like @bcnesports will crown the #VoltaCatalunya champions for the third year in a row. Barcelona’s experience combines art, music, Mediterranean cuisine, and cultural offerings. The city has more than 260 km of bike lanes, historic clubs, passionate fans, and a strong commitment to sustainable mobility. The long relationship between the #VoltaCatalunya and Barcelona has brought major cycling stars to the Catalan capital throughout history. Major cycling events have taken place in Barcelona, and this year @letourdefrance will also come two weeks later. L'Avinguda Maria Cristina will once again host the race finish, after wins by @ally_wollaston (2024) and @loesadegeest (2025) in reduced sprints. It will also crown the overall champion, following @demivollering and @mariannevosofficial, who will repeat. This Sunday, 21 June, join us for the finish of the #VoltaCatalunya in Barcelona.

item.title: Strong Tour Auvergne-Rhônes Alpes for Caja Rural-Seguros RGA, with José Félix Parra 9th and Jan Castellon 11th
item.description: Strong Tour Auvergne-Rhônes Alpes for Caja Rural-Seguros RGA, with José Félix Parra 9th and Jan Castellon 11th overall. On the final stage, Jan Castellon finished 13th and Parra 15th, again close to the main favorites on the final climb to Plateau de Solaison, a climb that will be included in the next Tour de France. Special mention to Alex Díaz, Sergi Darder, and Joan Bou for their strong work in support of their leaders. #tourauvergnrhonealpes #súmatealverde 💚

item.title: Thank you for a fantastic cycling festival, Copenhagen!
item.description: Thank you for a fantastic cycling festival, Copenhagen! This weekend, Copenhagen showed itself at its very best. Thousands of people joined the public cycling event before the world elite took to the streets. Children, families, and cycling enthusiasts rode the same route later used by Lorena Wiebes and Jasper Philipsen on their way to victory. It is special when a city can host some of the world's best athletes while also inviting everyone to take part. I am proud of the atmosphere Copenhageners created along the route. Once again, we showed why Copenhagen is one of the world's great cycling cities. Thank you to everyone who helped make Copenhagen Sprint a huge success.

item.title: A look toward the peaks of the new Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes trophy
item.description: A look toward the peaks of the new Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes trophy. item.title: Partner opportunities with Flanders Classics
item.description:
Looking for a partner platform that combines passion, visibility, and real engagement? At Flanders Classics, we organise some of the most iconic cyclo-cross competitions in the world, including the UCI Cyclo-cross World Cup and the Telenet Superprestige. These events bring millions of fans, extensive media exposure, and unique opportunities for brands to connect with highly engaged audiences.
From branded content and fan activations to recruitment campaigns and hospitality experiences, we create tailor-made collaborations that go beyond traditional sponsorship. As we continue to grow our CX portfolio and look ahead to the 2027 Cyclo-cross World Championships in Ostend, we are opening the door to new partnerships.
Interested in exploring opportunities? Download our partner brochure CX.

item.title: Valsir renews partnership with Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour
item.description: Valsir is once again a partner of the Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour for the fifth year in a row. The Italian company has been present on the Polish market for more than 20 years in the plumbing and sanitary systems sector. Like the race, it focuses on commitment, passion, and the pursuit of excellence. More about the partnership and preparations for the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour can be found on the website. #TDP2026 #TourdePologne #Valsir #PółnocPołudnie

item.title: @valsir_polska renews partnership with Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour

item.title: Preparations for the 83rd Tour de Pologne are in full swing
item.description: Preparations for the upcoming 83rd Tour de Pologne are in full swing. Meetings, discussions, and planning are all helping create an event that will once again combine sporting excitement with the promotion of Poland’s most beautiful places. Today included visits to TV Slupsk, where preparations for the race and the promotional potential of the region were discussed, and to the Słupsk County Governor Paweł Lisowski. During stage 1, Gdynia–Koszalin, a special prize of the Słupsk County Governor will be awarded in Kępice. The day also included a meeting with the First Deputy Mayor of Słupsk, Marta Makuch, with Jan Huruk and Rafał Szymański present. The day ended with a meeting with supporters in a Słupsk café. The final video features the talented violinist Oliwia Suchodolska. #TourdePologne

item.title: Our green initiatives for a more sustainable event
item.description: We’re sharing some of the things we do to create a greener event: upcycled T-shirts and tote bags for volunteers made from textiles from the BMX World Championships and other previous sports events; cooperation with New Loop and the use of washable packaging in the cycling festival and cheering zones; a food offering designed to reduce environmental and climate impact; replacing diesel generators with 100% city power and batteries; producing signs without dates so they can be reused next year; and water stations at the cycling festival where visitors can refill their own bottles.

item.title: Our green initiatives for a more sustainable event
item.description: We’re sharing some of the things we do to create a greener event: upcycled T-shirts and tote bags for volunteers made from textiles from the BMX World Championships and other previous sports events, produced by @heartbeatstudio.dk; cooperation with @newloopdk and the use of washable packaging in the Cycling Festival and cheering zones; a food offering designed to reduce environmental and climate impact; replacing diesel generators with 100% city power and batteries; producing signs without dates so they can be reused next year; and free drinking water stations at the Cycling Festival where visitors can refill their own bottles.

item.title: Traffic during Copenhagen Sprint affects North Zealand and the Copenhagen area
item.description: Traffic during Copenhagen Sprint will affect North Zealand and the Copenhagen area. In Copenhagen especially, there will be major traffic disruptions, and residents are encouraged to leave the car at home next weekend. More information about traffic in Copenhagen: https://copenhagensprint.com/trafikinfo/ More information about traffic in the start city Roskilde: https://copenhagensprint.com/trafikinforoskilde/ More information about traffic along the route: https://copenhagensprint.com/kommuner/ Roskilde Municipality, Copenhagen Municipality, Frederikssund Municipality, Fredensborg Municipality, Egedal Municipality, Hillerød Municipality, Furesø Municipality, Ballerup Municipality, Rødovre Municipality, Hvidovre Municipality, Brøndby Municipality, Allerød Municipality
