item.title: Mark Renshaw reflects on life after the peloton
item.description: He holds the record for the most starts in Santos Tour Down Under history, but these days Mark Renshaw is navigating the peloton from the front seat of a team car. Six years after retirement, the former world-leading lead-out specialist reflects on the move from fighting for position at 80 km/h to guiding the next generation of sprinters as a Sports Director for DECATHLON CMA CGM TEAM. Read more about life after the peloton for Mark Renshaw: https://bit.ly/4ubfUJe

item.title: Mark Renshaw reflects on life after the peloton
item.description: He holds the record for the most starts in Santos Tour Down Under history, but these days Mark Renshaw is navigating the peloton from the front seat of a team car. Six years after retirement, the former world-leading lead-out specialist reflects on the move from fighting for position at 80 km/h to guiding the next generation of sprinters as a Sports Director for @decathloncmacgmteam. “It still gets the heart rate up. I’m still very calm in the team car, especially in the final, as you’re still focused, but the heart rate and adrenaline still come up for sure.” “Sprinting hasn’t changed — the fundamentals are all the same and so much of it is about positioning, momentum and timing.” Read more about Mark Renshaw’s life after the peloton at the link in our bio. @santos_ltd #TourDownUnder

item.title: Mark Renshaw reflects on life after the peloton

item.title: Pogačar already at the front
item.description: We were expecting him, and he did not disappoint. @tadejpogacar did not completely dominate the competition — he even admitted he was glad not to be alone on the Ovronnaz climb — but his sprint victory was clear. The @uae_team_emirates leader will wear the yellow jersey from tomorrow, a jersey he says he intends to keep until Sunday evening. 📸 @keystone_sda_sport #TDR #TDRNONSTOP #ROMANDIE #TDR2026 @caffechiccodoro @loterieromande @banque_du_leman @decathlon_ch @lidltrek @primeo_energie

item.title: Tour de Romandie stage 1: Tadej Pogačar wins in Martigny
item.description: Did you miss the first stage of the Tour de Romandie around Martigny, the enthusiastic crowd for Slovenian star Tadej Pogačar, and his impressive ride on the Mayens-de-Chamoson climb? Although the four-time Tour de France winner could not drop all his rivals, he won the four-rider sprint in Martigny and became the fifth world champion to win there. Watch the stage highlights from RTS here: https://lnkd.in/ePn3DkVe And be sure to watch his press-room interview, where it was revealed that the champion’s parents were present on the roadside for this latest success. #TDR #TDRnonstop #Tdrinside #romandie #martigny

item.title: The #Radklassiker2026 is getting ready to start — and it is much more than just a cycling event!
item.description: Supporting young riders means shaping the future. With the Süwag Energie Nachwuchs-Cup, @suewagenergieag will once again support young cycling talents in 2026 and help them move forward on their path. 🌿 Experience the energy transition in the middle of the city The Süwag Energiepark at the Alte Oper invites visitors to experience the energy transition up close — interactive, easy to understand, and for the whole family. 🤝 Strong together for the region Süwag Energie AG shows how regional ties, sports support, and sustainability can go hand in hand. 💡 Curious? @suewagenergieag shares regular updates about the Radklassiker. Follow now so you do not miss anything. Radklassiker2026 #SüwagEnergieAG #Energie.Besser.Machen #Radsport #Nachwuchsförderung #GrüneZukunft #AlteOper

item.title: Our Spring Classics season has officially come to an end, and what a ride it has been
item.description: From the start of Omloop Nieuwsblad in Ghent to the iconic finish of the Ronde van Vlaanderen in Oudenaarde and all the way to the Cauberg in Valkenburg, every moment reminded us why this part of the cycling calendar is so special. We enjoyed it from the first minute to the very last, and we are grateful to everyone who helped make this season unforgettable. Relive some of the biggest highlights in our season recap via the link below. #FLCS #SetThePace

item.title: A season to remember 💛 | 2026 Spring Classics Recap
The @adac bicycle insurance is of course not missing the ADAC Velotour
item.description: At the stand on Frankfurt's Opernplatz, the ADAC bicycle insurance has a special highlight for young cycling fans. The main prize is a yellow woom GO 3 AUTOMAGIC children's bike for children aged 4 to 6, and second and third prizes are woom READY children's helmets in sizes XS, S, and M, each with a helmet light. How to enter: 1. Visit the ADAC bicycle insurance stand 2. Have your child's helmet size measured on site 3. Take part in the quiz, fill out the entry card, and keep your fingers crossed. The live draw takes place on May 1, 2026 at 5 p.m. at the stand. Stop by — you and your whole family are welcome! #VelotourFrankfurt #EschbornFrankfurt #ADAC #FahrradLiebe #Gewinnspiel #SicherRadfahren #FrankfurtCity

item.description: Experience the 15th edition of #GPCQM2026 from the Signature Space, the most sought-after spot on the circuit. What awaits you: a clear view of the start and finish, and gourmet offerings all day long. Limited places available, book soon. gpcqm.ca/hospitalite

item.title: Experience the 15th edition of GPCQM from the Signature Space
item.description: Experience the 15th edition of GPCQM from the most sought-after spot on the circuit: the Signature Space. On September 11 in Quebec and September 13 in Montreal, imagine yourself just a few meters from the world's best cyclists, in a unique atmosphere combining sporting intensity and attentive service. What awaits you: a clear view of the start and finish line, gourmet food and refreshments throughout the day, and an electric atmosphere to celebrate this anniversary edition. Places are limited. Reserve your experience: https://gpcqm.ca/hospitalite/ #gpcqm2026

item.title: Experience the 15th edition of the GPCQM from the circuit’s most sought-after spot: the Signature Area
item.description: Experience the 15th edition of the GPCQM from the circuit’s most sought-after spot: the Signature Area. On September 11 in Quebec City and September 13 in Montreal, picture yourself just a few meters from the world’s best cyclists, in a unique atmosphere that combines sporting intensity with attentive service and comfort. What to expect: 📍 An unbeatable view of the start and finish line. 🥂 Gourmet food and refreshments throughout the day. 🔥 An electric atmosphere to celebrate this anniversary edition. Places are limited. Reserve your experience via the link in bio! 🎟️ #gpcqm2026

item.title: The Tour of the Alps is over, but your shopping tour at Brenner Outlet continues all year round
item.description: The Tour of the Alps is over, but your shopping tour at Brenner Outlet continues all year round. Choose your outfit from more than 70 international brands in sport, outdoor fashion, and lifestyle. And don’t miss the new food court with restaurants, a pizzeria, and fast food. Like the #TotA, Brenner Outlet is located on the border between Italy and Austria, at the Brenner Pass! 📷 Sprint Cycling / Matassoni / Brenner Outlet #TouroftheAlps #LiveUphill #BrennerOutlet

item.title: More than a race: ADAC Velotour 2026 in facts and figures
item.description: More than a race: ADAC Velotour 2026 in facts and figures. 📊 12,000 participants – new record! 🎉 43% riding for the first time 👥 87% men / 13% women 💪 Ages 15 to 84 📈 Average age: 40.1 🌍 75 nationalities lined up from all over the world ⏳ Wishing you strong legs and perfect conditions for your final training sessions through the Taunus! We can’t wait to see you at the start line on May 1st! 🙌 #radklassiker #ADACVelotour #EschbornFrankfurt #roadcycling

item.title: Join the Social Ride during Copenhagen Sprint
item.description: Copenhagen Sprint is not only about the elite race, but also about community. As part of Copenhagen Sprint, we invite you to a Social Ride in Roskilde: a shared cycling experience of about 50 km for cycling enthusiasts. Riders will travel in smaller groups with experienced guides, and a service vehicle, mechanic, and refreshments will be provided along the way. The Social Ride is part of the race weekend and gives participants a chance to experience the atmosphere up close when Copenhagen Sprint gets underway. Early bird: 400 kr. until 8 May. Registration via the link in the comments. Roskilde Municipality.

item.title: Join the Social Ride during Copenhagen Sprint
item.description: Join a social ride of about 50 km with other cycling fans as part of the Copenhagen Sprint weekend in Roskilde. You will ride in smaller groups with experienced guides, and we will take care of the practical details along the way with a service vehicle, mechanic, and refreshments. It is all about great experiences, community, and a love of cycling. After the ride, you can experience the atmosphere up close when Copenhagen Sprint gets underway. Early bird: 400 kr. until 8 May. Sign up here: https://copenhagensprint.com/social-ride/

item.title: It was three in a row for @tadejpogacar at Liège-Bastogne-Liège
item.description: It was three in a row for @tadejpogacar at Liège-Bastogne-Liège, and this victory was especially hard fought. As Tadej brought his Classics campaign to a fitting end, we were there to uncover what it takes to win three Monuments in the same season. With the help of our star cameraman, @domen_novak1, our behind-the-scenes video from La Doyenne is not to be missed. #WeAreUAE | #LBL. Click the link in our bio for the full video!

A look back at Ardennes week with La Flèche Wallonne and Liège–Bastogne–Liège
item.description: A look back at an all-Ardennes week with La Flèche Wallonne and Liège–Bastogne–Liège, where the world’s best riders went head-to-head on legendary courses. In Huy, the Mur once again made the difference, producing an explosive showdown in both the men’s and women’s races, with victories for Paul Seixas and Demi Vollering. In Liège, La Doyenne lived up to expectations with panache, strategy, and decisive attacks all the way to the final kilometers, ending with wins for Tadej Pogačar and Demi Vollering. Relive the highlights in pictures. #Bostik #Cycling

item.title: Giveaway: win a Liège–Bastogne–Liège jersey signed by Demi Vollering and Tadej Pogačar
item.description: Giveaway: win a Liège–Bastogne–Liège jersey signed by Demi Vollering and Tadej Pogačar, winners of the 2026 editions. To enter: like this post, follow @classiquesardennes, and tag a friend in the comments. Winner announced on May 11. Good luck to everyone.

item.title: Liège–Bastogne–Liège 2026: photo recap
item.description: Liège–Bastogne–Liège 2026: photo recap. The 112th edition of La Doyenne once again delivered intense moments, combining performance, passion, and a huge public atmosphere. The Province of Liège was proud to support this iconic event at the heart of its territory and sporting identity. Congratulations to all the riders, and thank you to the many fans who came out to make the roads come alive. #liegebastogneliege #cyclisme #provincedeliege #sport #Liège

item.title: Tour of The Alps 2026 recap
item.description: Tour of The Alps 2026 recap. Five unforgettable days of racing, following the riders on the best roads that @euregio_europaregion has to offer. #tourofthealps #euregio #cycling #sportphotography #alps

item.title: Today we ride for something bigger
item.description: Together with the El Sueño de Vicky Foundation, which is dedicated to childhood cancer research, we visited the Hospital Infantil Universitario Niño Jesús to spend time with those who show us every day what true strength means. When a child faces cancer, it takes teamwork and nonstop effort to keep climbing. #LaVueltaesMás #MásSolidaria #ElSueñodeVicky

item.title: Wildcards for the 83rd Tour de Pologne have been awarded
item.description: The wildcards for the 83rd Tour de Pologne have been awarded. Alongside all UCI WorldTour teams, the race will also feature the strongest teams from outside the top tier: Q36.5 Pro Cycling Team, with Tom Pidcock and Kamil Małecki, and Tudor Pro Cycling Team. The team managed by Fabian Cancellara includes Julian Alaphilippe and Marc Hirschi. The Polish national team will also take part. The white-and-reds have once again been given the chance by Lang Team and Czesław Lang to race against the world’s best riders. From August 3 to 9, 2026, the world’s top riders return to Poland, and this year’s edition, under the theme “North–South,” will connect the Baltic Sea with the mountains. The race will be broadcast in up to 65 countries. #ORLEN #WygrywamyRazem

item.title: Brukunga wins 2026 Santos Best Dressed Town Overall Gold
item.description: Congratulations to the town of Brukunga, the 2026 Santos Best Dressed Town Overall Gold winner. Last week, Mount Barker District Council Mayor David Leach joined us to present the awards. Using sustainable, recycled, and handmade elements, from repurposed coffee pot planters to a bike sculpture made from salvaged Cudlee Creek bushfire timber, the town showed great creativity and community spirit. Well done to the Brukunga Community Association Inc. and the Mount Barker District Council. #TourDownUnder

item.title: Tour de Romandie 2026 route summary by Pascal Bärtschi
item.description: Pascal Bärtschi, the technical director of the #TDR, presents the key features of a very mountainous Tour de Romandie 2026. There is no time trial except for the prologue. With four stages run in loops, the race should provide great spectacle. The triple ascent of the Col du Jaun via three different roads will be historic. Full details are in the official program.

item.title: Demi Vollering wins Liège-Bastogne-Liège with a perfectly timed attack
item.description: Liège-Bastogne-Liège always delivers. It was a masterclass in timing, patience, and climbing power. After a tense race that split on the climbs, @demivollering attacked on La Redoute and never looked back. Behind her, the chase stalled as @puckpieterse and @kasianiewiadoma94 fought for the podium. Demi took her third win in Liège.

item.title: Two days until the TDR 2026 prologue in Villars-sur-Glâne
item.description: Two days before the TDR 2026 prologue, a look back to where it all began. Villars-sur-Glâne, Richard Chassot's childhood home, will host the symbolic start of the race. To mark the 20th TDR organized with his teams, this prologue is a return to the roots, linking past and present.

item.title: Tour of the Alps: a week to remember
item.description: Five stages, brutal mountains, and world-class racing. The team gave everything and looks back proudly on an incredible week. Emanuel Zangerle was crowned the Tour’s Breakaway King, while Colin Stüssi made a strong comeback to finish in the top 20 overall. The team also praised the commitment and spirit of every rider, the staff behind the scenes, the partners, and the fans on the roadside. The momentum continues: after the Tour is before the next race.

item.title: Tour of the Alps wrapped up
item.description: An important week of racing is in the books as the team builds toward the Giro.

item.title: Tour of the Alps, Stage 5: Trento to Bolzano
item.description: On the final stage of the Tour of the Alps, Red Bull – BORA – hansgrohe took the overall victory, with Giulio Pellizzari winning the stage. The rider also received a jersey from FC Südtirol and spotted sheep on the way to the finish with the Infront Sports crew.

item.title: A masterplan, perfectly executed
item.description: In the final stage of the Tour of the Alps, TEAM UKYO turned tactics into victory, with Tommaso Dati securing the red points jersey. Smart moves, teamwork, and commitment made the difference all week. Dati won the first intermediate sprint to take the lead in the points classification. Nicolo Garibbo then attacked from the break before the second and final intermediate sprint to take maximum points, limiting Tom Pidcock and putting Dati in control of the classification. When rivals dropped on the final climb, the plan worked perfectly. #TourOfTheAlps #TotA #TEAMUKYO

item.title: Tour of the Alps, Stage 5 and overall: Giulio Pellizzari wins
item.description: Giulio Pellizzari won the final stage of the Tour of the Alps and also claimed the overall victory, the first of his professional career. He finished 1:33 ahead of Egan Bernal, the largest gap between the top two in the race’s history. Since 2013, when Vincenzo Nibali won, no Italian had taken the overall title. Egan Bernal also earned a strong result, returning to the podium of a stage race after third place at the Volta Ciclista a Catalunya in 2023. Both Pellizzari and Bernal will line up at the Giro d’Italia, where they will continue their battle for the maglia rosa and a place on the final podium in Rome. Stage summary: a large breakaway formed early, including stage winners Lennart Jasch and Tom Pidcock, but was gradually caught. INEOS Grenadiers reduced the peloton before Giulio Pellizzari attacked 20 km from the finish and rode solo to victory, securing the overall title ahead of Egan Bernal and Thymen Arensman. Stage 5 podium: 1. Giulio Pellizzari (Red Bull - BORA - hansgrohe), 2. Egan Bernal (INEOS Grenadiers), 3. Michael Storer (Tudor Pro Cycling Team). Overall podium: 1. Giulio Pellizzari (Red Bull - BORA - hansgrohe), 2. Egan Bernal (INEOS Grenadiers), 3. Thymen Arensman (INEOS Grenadiers).

item.title: One month ago: E3 Saxo Classic
item.description: One month ago: the E3 Saxo Classic. Each year, I am able to contribute to one of Flanders' most beautiful cycling classics, from the briefing video for all WorldTour and junior teams to the aftermovie of the day itself. This year, once again, I was a guest at @ranson_nv, who opened their doors for the E3 organization. Thank you for the trust, a pleasure every year.

item.title: Tour of the Alps 2026 Stage 5: Trento - Bozen/Bolzano
item.description: April 24. Stage 5: Trento - Bozen/Bolzano, 128.6 km with 2,850 m of elevation gain. Difficulty: 3 stars. KOMs: Kalterer Höhe / Caldaro Alta (2nd category, km 54) and Nobls/Montoppio (1st category, km 93). Intermediate sprints: Palù di Giovo (km 11) and St. Pauls/San Paolo (km 63). Bonus sprint: Oberglaning/Cologna di Sopra (km 110). Start: 12:00. #TotA #TouroftheAlps #LiveUphill

item.title: Tour de Suisse 2026: Route of the women’s and men’s races
item.description: The Tour de Suisse 2026 features a clear route profile: explosive, selective, and without classic transition stages. The 89th edition of the national tour leaves little room for tactical stalemates and promises excitement from the first to the final kilometre. Day 1: Explosive start in Italy (Sondrio – Sondrio).

item.title: Tour de Suisse 2026: explosive stages and no transition days
item.description: The Tour de Suisse 2026 comes without transition stages: explosive stages, selective climbs, and excitement from start to finish. The 89th edition promises pure cycling. More on our website. #TourDeSuisse #TdS2026 #Cycling #Radsport #ProCycling

item.title: Flèche Wallonne: a race decided on the final climb
item.description: The Flèche Wallonne is a one-day cycling race that may look simple, but its challenge lies in the finale: a short, steep climb that forces riders to wait for the perfect moment. It is a test of pacing, discipline, and timing rather than pure strength. As the Official Technology Partner of the Flèche Wallonne (men's and women's) and 12 other races, Capgemini is helping shape the future of the sport. Cycle through the carousel to see what defines this race.

item.title: Czesław Lang sector on the route of stage 1
item.description: The Czesław Lang sector is on the route of stage 1. This year’s 83rd edition of the Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour is not only about top-level racing, but also a special journey through places close to race director Czesław Lang’s heart. On stage 1 from Gdynia to Koszalin, the riders will pass through a special Czesław Lang sector that runs through his home region. The route includes Parchowo, Gołczewo, Jeleńcz, Półczno, Łąkie, Mokrzyn, Mądrzechowo, Bytów - LOTTO intermediate sprint, Dąbie, Gostkowo, Kartkowo, Unichowo, Budowo, Motarzyno, Gałąźnia Wielka, Gałąźnia Mała, Świelubie, Górki, Wierszyno, Radusz, Barnowo, Kołczygłowy - Czesław Lang special sprint, Jezierze, and Suchorze. It is a scenic, rolling, and twisting section full of emotion, memories, and racing - a true tribute to a legend of Polish cycling. The main sponsor of the race is ORLEN. The strategic partner of the Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour is LOTTO.

item.title: Stage 1 of the 83rd Tour de Pologne with the Czesław Lang Sector
Eschborn-Frankfurt: more than 3,300 meters of climbing on a demanding course
item.description: At the #radklassiker Eschborn-Frankfurt, the elite face one of the toughest courses in recent years. The route demands everything from start to finish. New this year is Burgweg, a short, extremely steep and explosive climb. Key climbs: Sandplacken (1x) – 8 km, average gradient 4.6%; Burgweg (3x) – 0.5 km, average gradient 11%; Großer Feldberg (1st) – 3.5 km, average gradient 7.7%; Mammolshain (3x) – 2.3 km, average gradient 8.3%; Großer Feldberg (2nd) – 7.6 km, average gradient 6.5%. Feldberg is climbed twice via the southwest side over different distances, before the Mammolshain passage sets up a potentially decisive finale. Which section do you think will decide the race? #radklassiker #eschbornfrankfurt #procycling #readytorace

item.title: Leader jerseys and special prizes for the Tour de Romandie 2026
item.description: Who do you see winning the yellow jersey in the general classification of the Tour de Romandie? The stage and prologue winners will be rewarded by the new international sponsor of the Tour de Romandie, Lidl Suisse. Distinctive jerseys: orange JouezSport! jersey – Loterie Romande; white best young rider jersey – Banque du Léman; blue polka-dot best climber jersey – Primeo. What do you think of this new design? A competition on rider progression starts this Tuesday: put your cycling instincts to work. The best Swiss rider of the day receives the RTS prize. The most combative rider of the stage, first to pass the 25 km to go mark, receives the Chicco d’Oro prize. Your predictions? #TDR #TDRnonstop #TDRinside #romandie

item.title: Stage 2 of TotA 2026: uphill finish means showtime
Itzulia 2026 highlights
7 days to go: Tour de Romandie 2026 route presentation
item.description: The routes and profiles for Tour de Romandie 2026, totaling 851.3 km and 14,266 m of climbing, were presented this morning. Nearly 100 people attended, including many media representatives such as SRF - Schweizer Radio und Fernsehen for German-speaking Switzerland. This mountainous route, a record in the absence of a time trial apart from the prologue, is expected to suit a very strong rider. Who do you think will win the yellow jersey in the general classification of the Tour de Romandie? Stage winners and the prologue winner will be rewarded by the new international sponsor of the Tour de Romandie, Lidl Switzerland. Distinctive jerseys: orange JouezSport! - Loterie Romande jersey, white best young rider jersey sponsored by Banque du Léman, and blue polka-dot best climber jersey sponsored by Primeo Energie. A competition on rider progression starts this Tuesday. The best Swiss rider of the day receives the RTS - Radio Télévision Suisse prize. The most combative rider of the stage, first to pass the 25 km mark from the finish, receives the CHICCO D'ORO prize. What are your predictions?

item.title: Stage 1 of the 83rd Tour de Pologne featuring the Czesław Lang sector
item.description: On April 21, 2026, a special event was held at the Czesław Lang Primary School in Kołczygłowy, dedicated to the presentation of the Czesław Lang sector and the special prize named after him, which will be on the route of the first stage of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour.

item.title: Special event at the Czesław Lang Primary School in Kołczygłowy
item.description: Today, a special event was held at the Czesław Lang Primary School in Kołczygłowy to present the Czesław Lang sector and the special prize named after him, which will be on the route of the first stage of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour. The meeting is part of the intensive preparations for this year's Tour de Pologne, especially for stage 1, which will start in Gdynia and finish after 240 km in Koszalin, passing through Czesław Lang's home region. The special sector will include the following places: Parchowo, Gołczewo, Jeleńcz, Półczno, Łąkie, Mokrzyn, Mądrzechowo, Bytów (LOTTO intermediate sprint), Dąbie, Gostkowo, Kartkowo, Unichowo, Budowo, Motarzyno, Gałąźnia Wielka, Gałąźnia Mała, Świelubie, Górki, Wierszyno, Radusz, Barnowo, Kołczygłowy (Czesław Lang special prize), Jezierze and Suchorze. In the second part of the event, the students received a special surprise: the new school logo, funded by Czesław and Elżbieta Lang, was officially handed over. The ceremony presented the symbolism of the emblem and the story of its creation, followed by the official unveiling of the school crest.

item.title: Tour de Romandie 2026 route revealed in Villars-sur-Glâne
item.description: The Tour de Romandie has revealed the route for 2026 in Villars-sur-Glâne, where next Tuesday’s prologue will take place. The race will cover 851.3 km with 14,266 m of climbing. It is a very mountainous route, with no time trial except the prologue.

item.title: Tour de Romandie 2026 presentation this Tuesday, seven days before the prologue
item.description: The presentation conference will take place at 10:00. Maps, profiles, start list, and new features will be revealed. Follow along to learn everything about the 2026 Tour de Romandie and the riders who will challenge Tadej Pogačar. One figure about the Slovenian star: a single fan page has nearly 200,000 followers.

item.title: Paula Blasi surprises everyone! | Highlights Amstel Gold Race 2026
A surprise win and a strong return at last weekend's Amstel Gold Race
item.description: Last weekend's Amstel Gold Race featured a surprise win and a strong comeback. On the women’s side, Paula Blasi took the biggest victory of her career after being dropped on the Bemelerberg alongside Marianne Vos, then fighting back when the pace eased, attacking immediately, and never looking back. The chase lacked organisation. Katarzyna Niewiadoma and Demi Vollering were isolated in the finale and completed the podium after a battle on the Cauberg. On the men’s side, Remco Evenepoel and Mattias Skjelmose renewed their rivalry in an epic battle over the Dutch hills. Evenepoel sprinted to victory and claimed his first Dutch classic. This year’s Amstel Gold Race elite race was the last edition with race director Leo van Vliet, who will be succeeded by Tom Dumoulin after 30 years. Thank you to Leo and to all the partners who made the race possible: Amstel Bier, Shimano Benelux B.V., Lazer, Boels Rental, Immoweb, Staatsloterij, Wealer, Lidl Nederland, PostNL, Pauwels Sauces, Tudor Watch, and Dolmans Landscaping Group bv.

item.title: Soudal named official partner of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour
item.description: Soudal has become an official partner of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour. Last year, Paul Magnier took a stage win in Cieszyn for a team sponsored by Soudal. In Poland, the team raced in specially designed T-Rex Quick-Step kits promoting one of the brand's flagship products. The partnership underlines the importance of Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour, which is one of the key races on the UCI calendar and reaches millions of fans through broadcasts on Telewizja Polska, Eurosport, and HBO Max. Read more here: https://www.tourdepologne.pl/…/soudal-oficjalnym-partnerem…/ Soudal Polska Soudal #soudal #tourdepologne

item.title: Lidl-Trek confirms Jonathan Milan will start at Copenhagen Sprint
item.description: Lidl-Trek has confirmed that Jonathan Milan will start at Copenhagen Sprint. Milan is one of the world’s fastest sprinters and the winner of the points classification in the Tour de France 2025. Lidl-Trek arrives with clear ambitions, and it will be interesting to see him in action.

item.title: Polish riders will start again in Tour de Pologne Women
item.description: The 4th edition of Tour de Pologne Women will take place from 24 to 26 July and has been upgraded to UCI ProSeries, bringing an even higher level of competition and WorldTour teams. Among the starters will be the Polish National Team and MAT ATOM Deweloper Wrocław. It is a great chance for our riders to test themselves against the world’s best; last year Kaja Rysz and Malwina Mul finished in the top 10 overall. The race will be held in the Lublin region and will be broadcast on TVP, Eurosport, and HBO Max, available in 65 countries.

item.title: What better way to end our Spring Classics than De Brabantse Pijl! On the women’s side, strength did not guarantee victory. Anna van der Breggen looked like the strongest rider in the race, but it was Célia Gery who seized her opportunity. After already impressing in a supporting role this spring, she was given her chance in an unexpected sprint and delivered with confidence. The young French rider surged to her first professional road victory in style. On the men’s side, a strong elite group featuring Romain Grégoire and former winner Benoît Cosnefroy built a promising advantage after the final climbs, but just when it seemed the attackers would hold on all the way to Overijse, everything came back together in the final S-Bocht. Anders Foldager judged his effort perfectly and launched at exactly the right moment. He powered to the biggest win of his career, holding off Quinten Hermans and Cosnefroy in a thrilling sprint. Congratulations to all riders for an unforgettable day of racing, and a huge thank you to all partners, volunteers and supporters who made it possible. KBC Bank & Verzekering, Proximus Group, KPMG Belgium, Lidl België & Luxemburg, Lotto (Nationale Loterij), Alken-Maes, Eneco Belgium, Tudor Watch, BMW Belux, Kwaremont (Brouwerij De Brabandere), MyWhoosh, Lokaal bestuur Lennik, Lokaal Bestuur Beersel, Gemeente en OCMW Overijse, Sport Vlaanderen and Cycling in Flanders (Toerisme Vlaanderen - VISITFLANDERS). #FLCS #DBP26 #SetThePace

10 days until the prologue of the Tour de Romandie
item.description: The countdown is on: on Tuesday, April 21, the 2026 edition will be presented to partners and media. One name is already drawing attention: Tadej Pogačar. Before racing in Romandie, the Slovenian will still ride Liège-Bastogne-Liège, his last stop before going straight into his first Tour de Romandie. How will the two-time Paris-Roubaix runner-up arrive? One thing is certain: when Pogi is on the start line, there is something extra. See you in 10 days.

item.title: Stage 3 of the Women’s Tour: Cat Ferguson wins in Kelso
item.description: A landmark day in Kelso as Cat Ferguson claimed her first Women’s WorldTour victory on home soil and secured three jerseys. Dominika Włodarczyk also stood out in the mountains, taking the QOM title.

item.title: Luke Durbridge reflects on retirement and home
item.description: "It’s not the results, it’s the people." From the inaugural GreenEDGE roster in 2012 to becoming the heartbeat of the Australian peloton, Luke Durbridge has seen it all. After 20 years of chasing dreams across Europe, he is finally ready to head home. Durbo explains his decision to retire, the reality of midnight flights, and why there is no place like the Santos Tour Down Under.

item.title: “Adelaide is like a second home.” Luke Durbridge reflects on the Santos Tour Down Under
item.description: Luke Durbridge has raced the Santos Tour Down Under 12 times, won the Festival of Cycling, and shared in some of the race’s most iconic victories with his teammates. As he prepares for his final season, he explains why the Santos Tour Down Under remains his favourite stop on the calendar.

item.title: Tim Merlier wins the Ronde van Limburg in dominant sprint
item.description: After his victory in the Scheldeprijs, Tim Merlier confirmed his return to top form with a powerful win in the Ronde van Limburg. On the demanding finishing stretch in Tongeren-Borgloon, he launched his sprint with authority and quickly opened a clear gap on the field. Fernando Gaviria finished second, while Floris Van Tricht took third as the best of the rest.

item.title: 14 days to go until Stage 3: Rue - Vucherens
item.description: 14 days to go until Stage 3: Rue - Vucherens. The expression “through hills and valleys” dates back to the 15th century and fits this stage well. On Thursday, April 30, the route from Rue in the canton of Fribourg to Vucherens in the canton of Vaud will feature hills, village crossings, changes of pace, and typical landscapes. The third stage includes three 50 km loops in the Jorat, crossing the finish line three times and climbing the Vuillens hill, a third-category mountain prize, 3 km from the finish. The stage will feature riders such as Tadej Pogačar, Primož Roglič, Florian Lipowitz, and Lenny Martinez.

item.title: Exceptional TV audiences in Belgium
item.description: Exceptional TV audiences in Belgium: 88% audience share at the moment of Wout van Aert’s winning finish on VRT1, and a 47% average audience share on La Une for Paris-Roubaix. For Paris-Roubaix Femmes, VRT1 reached 75% audience share at the moment of Franziska Koch’s finish, also a record, while La Une recorded a 23.4% average audience share, another record.

item.title: FLCS Clubhouse presented by MyWhoosh brings sport, communities and brands together in Ghent
item.description:
Last week, we launched the first FLCS Clubhouse presented by MyWhoosh at the Wintercircus in Ghent. Over five days, the venue became an open sports hub where athletes, communities and brands came together to explore how sport looks today and where it is heading.
Built around the theme “Beyond the Club”, the Clubhouse highlighted a shift toward more flexible, digital and community-driven ways of engaging with sport. From talks to activations, the focus was on creating connections that go beyond traditional structures, both online and offline.
The week featured social rides and runs across the city, including Salomon runs that brought runners of all levels together. On the cycling side, the Sportful ride with Peter Sagan, Paolo Bettini and Daniel Oss was a standout moment.
Inside the Clubhouse, Rite.Studios hosted immersive sessions combining performance and recovery. The space also became a meeting point for ideas, collaboration and storytelling, where sport, lifestyle and innovation came together.
The Lidl België & Luxemburg Fresh Table dinner further showed the power of bringing people together, on and off the bike.
With the FLCS Clubhouse, Flanders Classics takes a step forward from event organiser to a platform connecting communities, partners and athletes in a hybrid sports ecosystem.
Thanks to our partners: MyWhoosh, 100%, Salomon, Sportful, Neversecond Benelux, La Marzocco, Red Bull, WOWOW Reflective Wear and Lidl.
And to the communities who helped shape this experience: Ghent Running Crew, RPM™ Souplesse, Octane Racing and Rite Studios.
This was just the beginning. #SetThePace #FLCS

item.title: Copenhagen Sprint evolves for 2026
item.description: Copenhagen Sprint is evolving. In 2026, the race will introduce the world’s first cargo bike caravan on the Copenhagen circuit, inspired by the Tour de France but reimagined for a true cycling city. Danish national teams will return, and the route has now been revealed.

item.title: Route for Copenhagen Sprint 2026 announced
item.description: The route for Copenhagen Sprint 2026 has now been published. The race starts in Roskilde, passes through the Zealand countryside and North Zealand, and finishes with an intense finale on the circuit in Copenhagen. Two days of world-class racing await.

item.title: Route for Copenhagen Sprint 2026 announced
item.description: The route for Copenhagen Sprint 2026 has been announced. It starts at Stændertorvet in Roskilde and finishes with the final lap in the heart of Copenhagen.

item.title: Route for Copenhagen Sprint 2026 announced
item.description: The route for Copenhagen Sprint 2026 has been announced. It starts at Stændertorvet in Roskilde and finishes with the final lap in the heart of Copenhagen.

item.title: 2026 route announced: start in Roskilde and finish with laps in Copenhagen
item.description: The 2026 route starts in Roskilde at Stændertorvet, goes through North Zealand, and ends with finishing laps in Copenhagen. The women will ride 3 laps and the men 5 laps on the Copenhagen circuit. This year, the riders enter via Vigerslev Allé and join the Copenhagen circuit at Rådhuspladsen by the Faxe-kondi corner, making the celebration there even bigger. Once again, the men’s route includes a special highlight and crosses Kronprinsesse Marys Bro in Frederikssund. We look forward to seeing the world’s best riders race through Zealand again. Mark 13–14 June in your calendar when the WorldTour comes to Denmark.

item.title: Broc–Charmey: a stage full of challenges
item.description: The Saturday, May 2 stage clearly bears the Bärtschi family’s touch. Bernard, the father, dreamed of this route from the chocolate village. Before finding the solution, he passed the role of technical director to his son Pascal. A small road spotted during a holiday ultimately allowed the new route master to design this queen stage: three climbs of the Jaun, via three different roads. The finale is brutal, with 700 m of elevation gain at 8 to 12%. It is a perfect terrain for a punchy climber looking to make a big move, in the style of Tadej Pogacar. For Switzerland, attention will also be on the Jura rider Yannis Voisard. The challenge will also be logistical, with many roads and access points closed throughout the region. Spectators are advised to arrive in Charmey early in the morning. The last three finishes in the Jogne valley: 2002: Dario Frigo won in Charmey and laid the foundations for his overall victory, ahead of Alex Zülle. 2007: Matteo Bono won from a three-rider breakaway. Thomas Dekker won the overall ahead of Paolo Salvoldelli. 2012: Luis León Sánchez won the stage in a Tour de Romandie won by Bradley Wiggins. Who do you think will win the stage this year?

item.title: Official graphic for the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour
item.description: We present the official graphic for the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour. On 3 August 2026, the eyes of the cycling world will turn to Poland. This special edition will start in Gdynia, which is celebrating its 100th anniversary this year, and from there the race will begin a great sporting journey across the country. This year’s route will take the peloton from north to south, from the Baltic Sea coast to demanding mountain stages in southern Poland. It will be a week full of speed, excitement, and spectacular racing. We can expect unforgettable views, major sporting moments, and an atmosphere felt at every kilometer of the route. 3–9 August 2026. This will be a race not to be missed.

item.title: Chaos on the cobbles at Paris-Roubaix
item.description: Chaos on the cobbles. Dust, crashes, and split seconds. Across both the men’s and women’s races, @rideshimano Neutral Service was right in the middle of it. 12 wheel swaps, 1 bike change under pressure, 2 chain drops fixed in seconds, 6 riders back on their bikes after crashes, and 4 bidons handed out along the way. Paris-Roubaix at its toughest. #RideShimano #DURAACE

item.title: Do you know the Club Maillot Jaune?
item.description: Joining this circle is not just about enjoying a VIP experience at the Tour de Romandie. It means becoming part of a privileged network of partners and gaining access to an influential community around the Boucle romande. It offers a unique immersion in the race, from following a rider during the prologue to rare moments such as seeing Tadej Pogacar ahead of the peloton (#TDRF2025). It also includes an exceptional gastronomic experience with the gourmet stage on 19 June at Fairmont Le Montreux Palace. Membership opens the door to valuable meetings, business opportunities and synergies, and exclusive events throughout the year. Designed by Laurent CLAUDE for decision-makers, entrepreneurs and cycling fans, the club combines sport, gastronomy and strategic networking. It also supports the Tour de Romandie and helps promote cycling and the Romandie region. Annual membership: CHF 1,000. Limited places. Interested in this exclusive circle? Get in touch. It is still time before #TDR2026.

item.title: Stefan Bissegger finishes 8th after late attack at Paris-Roubaix
item.description: With 4 kilometers to go, the strategy in the team car was clear: “We risk everything for the podium.” Stefan Bissegger gave it his all, but the velodrome had the final say. He finished 8th. #DECATHLONCMACGMTEAM

item.title: Franziska Koch wins the 6th edition of @parisroubaix_femmes
item.description: Franziska Koch won the 6th edition of @parisroubaix_femmes after 143 km of racing and 33.7 km of cobbles. She beat Marianne Vos in a thrilling finish, with Pauline Ferrand-Prévot still close behind. The iconic finish at the Vélodrome André-Pétrieux once again delivered a dramatic finale. Podium: 1. Franziska Koch 2. Marianne Vos 3. Pauline Ferrand-Prévot #hdf #parisroubaix #parisroubaixfemmes #sport

item.title: A hard day on the cobbles at Paris-Roubaix
item.description: On the cobbles of @parisroubaixcourse in France, there are no easy moments—only grit, chaos, and the relentless will to keep pushing. We fought for every meter of the Hell of the North and finished second on the podium after a hard-earned race. #WeAreUAE #ParisRoubaix

item.title: A cold shower after a hard day’s work
item.description: There is nothing better than a cold shower after a hard day’s work. Bad luck at Ahrenberg with a puncture; there I lost contact with the front group. In the end, a solid 31st place in my 13th @parisroubaixcourse, and my fastest race as well. After the shower, the @tc_parisroubaixjuniors organized a surprise party at the @amisdeparisroubaix museum for my family, my friends, and me. Thank you so much for this one. It’s always great to be here, chez mes amis, a Roubaix. Hugs #dege #dgnklb 📷 @mr.pinko

item.title: Finishing @parisroubaixcourse means calling your dad from the showers
item.description: This is what it means to finish @parisroubaixcourse… When you’re in the showers of the Roubaix Velodrome, you have to FaceTime your dad, and idol. 🥰

Orbe-Orbe stage will feature Danilo Wyss
item.description: Thanks to Club Maillot Jaune for reminding us that the Orbe-Orbe stage will feature Danilo Wyss. He will have several roles, both on the local organizing committee and in the #TDR caravan as VIP car manager. Interested in experiencing the Tour de Romandie from the inside? Join the Club Maillot Jaune team led by Laurent CLAUDE. #tdr #tdrinside #tdrnonstop #clubmaillotjaune #TDRVIP

item.title: Pogačar and other stars should like the Martigny stage
item.description: Alpine scenery, a looped route, and in the final lap the climb of the Mayens de Chamoson, which could suit a big attack. The stage also carries a lot of history: the previous 12 finishes of the Tour de Romandie in Martigny have produced memorable results. Great riders have shone there, including on the 1953 Tour de Romandie when Koblet won overall and Bahamontes and Coppi finished second. Four world champions have won in Martigny, including Mario Cipollini in 1996 and Peter Sagan in 2021, after Darrigade and Bobet. Pogačar could become the fifth. The VC Excelsior Martigny, organizer of the stage on 29 April, shares more information on its website.

item.title: Countdown to the Orbe - Orbe stage and Villars-sur-Glâne prologue
item.description: 21 days to the Orbe - Orbe stage and 18 days to the prologue in Villars-sur-Glâne. A great day awaits both the riders and the public. After the start, the peloton will cross the finish line three times during the stage, which should keep the atmosphere going right up to the finish. It could end in a sprint, making it the best day for the sprinters' teams to try something. There is also a surprise in the sky over Orbe at the start. Mini-quiz: which rider, while ill, missed his third Tour de Romandie victory in an individual time trial in Orbe in 1996, when Abraham Olano won overall? #TDR2026 #tdrinside #tdrnonstop #romandie #romandieforever #boucleromande

Scheldeprijs 2026 in Schoten: Charlotte Kool and Tim Merlier win
item.description: A very sunny edition of the Scheldeprijs 2026, with perfect weather for the 100th edition in Schoten. In the women’s race, seven riders went clear early, but the breakaway was caught well before the finish and the peloton stayed in control on the local laps. Charlotte Kool confirmed her status as top favourite with an impressive and composed performance, finally taking her long-awaited victory in Schoten after several near misses in previous editions. In the men’s race, Tim Merlier delivered a powerful sprint. A key moment came when Jordi Meeus opened his sprint early, briefly boxing Jasper Philipsen in. Merlier took the opportunity on the outside and surged past the field to win once again. #SetThePace #SP26 #FLCS

item.title: A decade since Mathew Hayman’s Paris-Roubaix win
item.description: It has been a decade since Mathew Hayman lifted the iconic stone at Paris-Roubaix, yet his race-winning bike remains untouched, a silent witness to 255 kilometres of dust and cobbles. Ten years on from that career-defining Sunday, the 12-time Santos Tour Down Under starter reflects on his journey from lifelong domestique and road captain to his 'holy grail' win and eventual move to Sports Director for GreenEdge Cycling. He says the 2016 Paris-Roubaix fulfilled a career, but that he would have done the sport all over again even without the win. He also reflects that the journey matters more than the result, because as great as the victory was, it faded more quickly than 19 years in the sport.

item.title: We’re back on the road. Numbers are our specialty, so we know that 13 brings luck, especially when we join Tour de Pologne for the 13th year in a row as Strategic Partner.
item.description: We’re back on the road. Numbers are our specialty, so we know that 13 brings luck, especially when we join Tour de Pologne for the 13th year in a row as Strategic Partner of this special event. Ahead of us is the 83rd edition of the only UCI WorldTour race in Central and Eastern Europe. As every year, an important part of our partnership remains the popular LOTTO Most Active Rider classification, which has long added pace, tension, and excitement from the very start of the race. We can’t wait. It will be #odLOTTOwo!

item.title: LOTTO stays with us for longer. The LOTTO brand, part of Totalizator Sportowy, will remain Strategic Partner of Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour and Partner of the LOTTO Most Active Rider classification for the next two years.
item.description: LOTTO stays with us for longer. The LOTTO brand, part of Totalizator Sportowy, will remain Strategic Partner of Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour and Partner of the LOTTO Most Active Rider classification for the next two years. Czesław Lang says that Totalizator Sportowy has been with us for 13 years, and the blue LOTTO Most Active Rider jersey has become a familiar sight for fans, delivering plenty of excitement every year. See you on the route of the 83rd edition from 3 to 9 August.

item.title: An incredible day at the Ronde van Vlaanderen
item.description:
Tadej Pogacar confirmed his status as the strongest rider in the world with a commanding and fearless performance in the Ronde van Vlaanderen. He animated the race from the first passage on the Oude Kwaremont, controlled the pace, and then launched his decisive move on the final ascent. Remco Evenepoel and Mathieu van der Poel fought bravely but had no answer to Pogacar’s accelerations. Pogacar rode solo all the way to Oudenaarde to secure a third victory, placing him among the race’s record holders.
In the women’s race, Demi Vollering delivered a masterclass to claim her first victory in the Tour of Flanders. Perfectly supported by her team, she attacked on the Oude Kwaremont with an acceleration no one could match. She then extended her lead with authority to seal an unforgettable win and add another major title to her palmarès.
Thank you to all riders, teams, and fans for another unforgettable edition of the Ronde van Vlaanderen.

item.title: All signals green, three times through Schoten with the Manx Express
FLCS and Proximus present Event Reservation
Proximus supports Flanders Classics with Event Reservation for race communication
item.description: With the support of partner Proximus, Flanders Classics will use Event Reservation this spring to facilitate communication between the peloton and the race control centre. The technology reserves part of the available mobile network capacity for crucial race communication and marks a new step in the safety measures implemented during Flanders Classics races. Tomas Van Den Spiegel, CEO of Flanders Classics, said that the race control centre is the central hub where all information from the race comes together, including monitoring by the federal police, but that the connection from there to race management and the safety team along the course is not always easy. Large crowds along the course using the same mobile network can lead to congestion at critical points. Thanks to Proximus, Flanders Classics can now use Event Reservation, which gives priority to mission-critical communication on the mobile network and helps ensure the connection between all actors. Kris van Cauwenbergh, Network Expert at Proximus NXT, said Proximus is proud to support Flanders Classics in ensuring safety on and around the course. Event Reservation secures crucial race communication, even during peak network usage. The technology has already been used during Omloop het Nieuwsblad, In Flanders Fields – From Middelkerke to Wevelgem, and Dwars door Vlaanderen, and will also be deployed during the Tour of Flanders, Scheldeprijs, and De Brabantse Pijl.

item.title: Dwars door Vlaanderen delivered once again
item.description: Dwars door Vlaanderen delivered once again. The Flemish classic was a race of grit, timing, and relentless ambition, and a true midweek highlight on the road to the Tour of Flanders. In the men’s race, Wout van Aert came heartbreakingly close to victory after an all-in effort. He launched a powerful solo attack with more than 40 kilometers to go, broke up the field on climbs like the Eikenberg, and bridged to the leaders with authority. One by one, his rivals dropped, leaving van Aert alone against the peloton. But in the final meters, Filippo Ganna surged past to deny van Aert the win. In the women’s race, Marlen Reusser marked her return in impressive fashion. After months away, she took victory through strength and race intelligence. Together with Demi Vollering, she forced a decisive move at the front and built a gap that seemed enough to decide the race. Late attacker Lieke Nooijen bridged back in the final straight, creating a moment of hesitation. Thank you to all riders, teams, and fans for another unforgettable edition of Dwars door Vlaanderen. KBC Bank & Verzekering, Proximus Group, KPMG Belgium, Lidl België & Luxemburg, Lotto (Nationale Loterij), Alken-Maes, Eneco Belgium, Tudor Watch, BMW Belux, Kwaremont (Brouwerij De Brabandere), MyWhoosh, Stad Roeselare, Stad Waregem, Sport Vlaanderen, and Cycling in Flanders (Toerisme Vlaanderen - VISITFLANDERS). #DDV26 #FLCS #SetThePace

item.title: Tour de Romandie newsletter: 26 days to go
item.description: The official Tour de Romandie newsletter will be published this Thursday. This edition highlights: the arrival of Lidl Suisse as fresh food partner and stage-winning sponsor; the best climber game (white jersey with blue dots) by Primeo Energie; the 2026 teaser; the immersive experience offered to VIP guests; and key partners such as Orange Cyberdefense Switzerland (Wi-Fi 7) and Swiss Wine Promotion. Beyond the competition, this UCI WorldTour race is a platform that combines sport and business, hospitality and regional roots. Subscribe to the newsletter to learn more about Tour de Romandie and what happens behind the scenes. All editions are available online.

item.description: Filippo Ganna finished first, with Wout van Aert second and Soren Waerenskjold third in Dwars door Vlaanderen Men.

item.description: With 1 km to go, Wout van Aert leads by 3 seconds.

item.title: Dwars door Vlaanderen Men: Vermeersch and Ganna are 8 seconds behind Van Aert with 3 km to go
item.description: At 3 km to go, Florian Vermeersch and Filippo Ganna are 8 seconds behind Wout van Aert.

item.title: Dwars door Vlaanderen Men: Final cobbled sector completed with Van Aert still in front
item.description: The final cobbled sector of the day is done. Wout van Aert is still leading.

item.title: Dwars door Vlaanderen Men: Counterattack by Dries De Bondt with 7 km to go
item.description: Dries De Bondt launches a counterattack with 7 km remaining.

item.title: Dwars door Vlaanderen Men: Wout van Aert goes solo on Nokereberg with 9 km to go
item.description: Wout van Aert attacks on Nokereberg and goes solo, with the peloton 13 seconds behind.

item.description: INEOS Grenadiers and Soudal Quick-Step work together in the peloton as the gap comes down.

item.title: Dwars door Vlaanderen Men: The race comes back together with 14 km to go
item.description: The race is back together behind the two leaders, who have a 24-second lead.

item.description: Filippo Ganna and Laurence Pithie join the chasing group.

item.description: Race situation on Herlegemstraat: 1) Van Aert and Larsen. 2) Gregoire, Sheffield, Florian Vermeersch and T. van Dijke, 26 seconds behind. 3) Peloton, 44 seconds behind.

item.title: DDVmen - 30 km: Race situation on Doorn
item.description: DDVmen - 30 km: 1) Van Aert, Gregoire and Larsen 2) Sheffield and T. van Dijke (+22") 3) Peloton, controlled by Soudal Quick-Step (+37").

item.title: Another blistering attack from Wout van Aert on the Eikenberg
item.description: Another blistering attack from Wout van Aert on the Eikenberg, showing pure explosiveness.

item.description: DDVmen - 38 km: Van Aert replaced Gachignard at the front. He is joined by Gregoire and Mouris in the lead.

item.title: Dwars door Vlaanderen Men - 40 km: Wout van Aert attacks on Eikenberg and is alone
item.description: At 40 km, Wout van Aert attacked on Eikenberg and is riding alone in Dwars door Vlaanderen Men.

item.description: At 42 km, the three leaders are working well together as they increase their advantage.

item.title: Diest to host the Grand Départ of the Renewi Tour 2026
item.description: The 21st edition of the Renewi Tour will start in Diest on Wednesday, August 19, 2026. The city of the Demer will host the Grand Départ of the five-day UCI WorldTour race, with both the start and finish of the opening stage taking place in Diest. item.title: The Paterberg: a decade of heroics | Ronde van Vlaanderen (2010-2019)
Koszalin will host the finish of Stage 1 of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour
item.description: Koszalin will host the finish of Stage 1 of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour. On 3 August, the peloton will start in Gdynia and finish in Koszalin after a route through Pomerania. A dynamic and spectacular finale is expected. Today's conference included Tomasz Sobieraj, Mayor of Koszalin, and Czesław Lang, General Director of the race. #tourdepologne #koszalin #wyscig

item.title: Wiebes becomes sole record holder | Highlights In Flanders Fields - In Wevelgem 2026
A historic day at In Flanders Fields: three in a row for Lorena Wiebes
item.category: In Flanders Fields - from Middelkerke to Wevelgem (Instagram)
item.description: A historic day at In Flanders Fields: three in a row for Lorena Wiebes. Where will she stop?

item.title: In Flanders Fields delivered a thrilling finish in Wevelgem
item.description: In Flanders Fields delivered a thrilling finish in Wevelgem. Mathieu van der Poel and Wout van Aert were caught by the peloton after a fierce attack on the Kemmelberg. Alec Segaert bridged to the two leaders, but all three were caught before the finish. Jasper Philipsen won the men’s race in a sprint. In the women’s race, Lorena Wiebes controlled the final group, forced the decisive selection on the Kemmelberg, and timed her sprint perfectly to beat Fleur Moors. Congratulations to all riders and thank you to the partners, volunteers and supporters who made the day possible.

From LABORAL Kutxa and the Laboral Kutxa Euskadi team, we will always stand with the women and men who fight for their dreams, and against those who try to crush them. This team has always defended that women should have the place they deserve at the top level, and comments and behavior like these show that there is still a long way to go. #Matria #laluchasigue Mar 28, 2026

item.title: E3 climb challenge with HLN: Jonas De Fruytier tops the leaderboard
item.description: We partnered with the legendary E3 climb and HLN for a three-week challenge on the E3 climb, where everyone could try to set the fastest time. Today those times were compared with the pros. All results are available on Startracking: https://www.star-tracking.be/eventresult/1051 Congratulations to overall winner Jonas De Fruytier. A great period that got many people back on their bikes with joy.

Grupa Transportowa remains the logistics partner of Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour and all Lang Team races
item.description: Grupa Transportowa remains the logistics partner of Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour and all Lang Team races. This is a continuation of a long-standing partnership that has supported the organization of the biggest stage race in this part of Europe for years. Behind the smooth running of the race stands a huge logistics operation: thousands of kilometers, hundreds of locations, and the work of many people, all synchronized with the rhythm of the peloton. Tomasz Macherowski, President of Grupa Transportowa, says: “This is more than sponsorship for us - it’s an annual operational test in real conditions.”

item.title: RT by @Milano_Sanremo: The best news we could receive
item.description: The best news we could receive. After several days marked by caution and constant medical monitoring, Debora Silvestri has improved and can continue her recovery outside the hospital. After six days in hospital, Debora has been discharged and is on her way home, where she will continue recovering with her family and supported by the whole team. The team medical staff will remain by her side throughout her recovery, always prioritizing the best possible progress. Forza Debora #LaboralKutxaEuskadi

item.title: Not just cycling, a place for everyone. Everyone’s Classics
The most iconic week in cycling is almost here
item.description: The most iconic week in cycling is almost here. The cobbles, the climbs, the history: Flemish cycling week is about to begin. It is a moment where tradition meets passion and where the sport connects generations of fans around the world. This year, we look at these races through a different lens. They do not belong to just a few; they belong to everyone, from lifelong fans to new audiences, from elite riders to everyday cyclists. The spirit of the Classics is something we all share. That is why these races are Everyone’s Classics. They reflect the values we cherish: inclusion, diversity, and the universal love of cycling. No matter who you are or where you come from, there is a place for you on every road. #SetThePace #FLCS

item.title: Milano-Sanremo is more than a race
item.description: #Adv La @milanosanremo_ non è solo una gara, è una lezione. Una corsa che sulla carta sembra semplice… ma che in realtà è la più difficile da interpretare. Perché un atleta puoi arrivare perfetto dopo ore di fatica e poi giocarsi tutto negli ultimi 5 km. La Cipressa, il Poggio di Sanremo, la tensione che sale, il gruppo che si muove... È questo che rende questa corsa una Classica Monumento. E viverla nel dietro le quinte per me è stata un’emozione incredibile. #MilanoSanremo

item.title: Tour de Pologne once again main partner of PEKAO Bike Expo and the National Bike Test 2026
item.description: Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour is once again the main partner of PEKAO Bike Expo and the National Bike Test 2026, an event that has become a permanent fixture in the Polish cycling calendar. On April 11-12, PGE Narodowy in Warsaw will bring together the whole cycling community, from enthusiasts to professionals. The program includes equipment tests, talks with experts, and meetings with peloton stars such as Rafał Majka, Fabio Aru, and Czesław Lang. There will also be a special offer on entry packages for the ORLEN Lang Team Race series. Visit the stand, as quantities are limited. See you in Warsaw 🚴‍♂️

item.title: VIP places for Copenhagen Sprint are now on sale
item.description: VIP places for Copenhagen Sprint are now on sale. When Copenhagen Sprint takes over Copenhagen, it will be a day of high intensity, strong performances, and a special atmosphere in the city streets. Want to experience the race from the front row? In our VIP zone, you get an exclusive setting for the day, ideal for networking and great experiences with colleagues, customers, or partners. If you want your own area, we also offer private VIP setups, where you can invite customers or employees to a unique day around Copenhagen Sprint. Available for a minimum of 100 people. Contact: Mathias Pedersen, mathias.pedersen@cyklingdanmark.dk Read more here: https://lnkd.in/e8xJu3Ej

item.title: What comes to mind when I hear SANREMO? The energy of a race that made history
item.description: The first thing that comes to mind when I hear SANREMO? The sea? The Festival? No. The energy of a race that made history. 🚴🏻‍♀️ This weekend I experienced @milanosanremo_ up close, and yes... I was there mainly for Sanremo Women 🩷🌸 It returned last year after more than 20 years. Today I can tell you about it as it deserves. Between backstage, the race, and stolen moments, I saw up close what it really means to be there: the focus, the determination, the relentless pace. And then there is that emotion that is hard to explain, the kind that stays with you even when everything is over ✨ What it left me with: chills, adrenaline, pure life. And maybe that is exactly the point. We do not need to 'discover' women's sport, but finally start recognizing it. #MilanoSanremo #SanremoWomen

item.title: The power behind a Cipressa record
item.description: 8.1 km after crashing at Milano-Sanremo, and after a hard chase, Tadej Pogačar distanced everyone except Tom Pidcock and Mathieu Van der Poel with a fierce acceleration on the Cipressa. He set a new record for the 5.7 km climb in 8:50 and then won the race in a sprint against Pidcock.

item.title: Daryl Impey on his connection to Australia and life after the peloton
item.description: He is the only men's rider in history to win back-to-back at the Santos Tour Down Under, but Daryl Impey's connection to Australia goes much deeper than the podium. From winning the Ochre Jersey to now calling Australia home, Daryl shares how the TDU played a defining role in his career and his transition from professional cyclist to Sports Director for INEOS Grenadiers. Read more about life after the peloton for this honorary Aussie at the link in our bio. @santos_ltd #TourDownUnder

item.title: Tadej Pogačar wins Milano-Sanremo 2026 in a sprint finish
item.description: A sprint for the history books. Tadej Pogačar delivered huge numbers in a nail-biting final sprint to beat Tom Pidcock and take victory at Milano-Sanremo 2026. ⏱️ Time: 14“ 💨 Speed: 56.4 km/h 🌪️ Max speed: 59.0 km/h ⚡ Power: 1060 w 💥 Max power: 1250 w

item.title: The #MilanoSanremo and #SanremoWomen podcast on @53x11Podcast
item.description: The podcast about #MilanoSanremo and #SanremoWomen on @53x11Podcast. The epic story of cycling: the crash, the comeback, the sprint, the victory. Tadej Pogacar is king in Sanremo. From the SD Worx deck this year comes the Kopecky card: Lotte is back as Lotte.

item.title: The #MilanoSanremo and #SanremoWomen podcast on 53x11 Podcast
item.description: The podcast about #MilanoSanremo and #SanremoWomen on 53x11 Podcast. The epic story of cycling: the crash, the comeback, the sprint, the victory by a few centimeters. Tadej Pogacar is king in Sanremo. From the SD Worx deck this year comes the Kopecky card: Lotte is back as Lotte.

item.title: Update on @KNiewiadoma after the #Sanremowomen race
item.description: Kasia was assessed by our team doctor and sustained multiple cuts and bruises. We will continue to monitor her recovery closely. We wish Kasia, along with everyone else involved in today's crashes, a smooth and speedy recovery.

item.title: Got 165 on my FTP test yesterday. I must have gone wrong somewhere. #MilanoSanremo @CA_Ita
item.description: Got 165 on my FTP test yesterday. I must have gone wrong somewhere. #MilanoSanremo @CA_Ita Velon CC reported a brutal attack to drop MVDP. Tadej Pogačar delivered a huge attack on the Poggio that only Tom Pidcock was able to follow as Mathieu Van der Poel was distanced. Speed: 40.8 km/h. Max speed: 44.2 km/h. Power: 620 w. Max power: 890 w. Average gradient: 4.7%.

item.title: Official: team presentation for the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour will take place aboard ORP Błyskawica in Gdynia
item.description: The team presentation for the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour will take place aboard the historic ORP Błyskawica in Gdynia on August 2, 2026. The event will combine the excitement of sport with Poland’s maritime history.

item.title: Official: 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour team presentation will take place on board the historic ORP Błyskawica in Gdynia
item.description: The team presentation for the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI World Tour will take place in a special location: on board the historic ORP Błyskawica in Gdynia. On 2 August 2026, the race will be launched in a setting that combines sporting excitement with Poland’s maritime history. We thank General Marek Sokołowski, Commander-in-Chief of the Polish Armed Forces, for approving and supporting this idea, and Commander Paweł Ogórek, the captain of ORP Błyskawica, for his involvement in organising the event. We also thank the authorities of the Polish Naval Academy for their work on preparing the presentation. Special thanks go to Alain Mompert, Honorary Consul of France in Pomerania and long-time Tour de Pologne Ambassador, for his logistical support in making this possible. It will be a special moment and a great opening to the Tour de Pologne.

item.title: Throwback to Men’s Stage 1 of the 2025 Tour of Britain
item.description: This week we’re looking back at Men’s Stage 1 of the 2025 Tour of Britain. Olav Kooij opened the week with a strong stage win, while Milan Lanhove made an early impact by taking two jerseys at the end of day one.

item.title: Meet our FLCS Clubhouse
item.description: From 2 to 6 April, Flanders Classics opens the doors of its FLCS Clubhouse presented by MyWhoosh for the first time at Wintercircus Ghent. It will be a place where sport and lifestyle come together during the week of the Tour of Flanders. The programme includes indoor cycling, running, Pilates and much more. Social runs, rides and virtual challenges are not the first things that come to mind when you think of Flanders Classics. In the sporting world, our company is still largely synonymous with professional cycling, the Flemish spring classics and cyclo-cross. With this initiative, we want to show that we are aware of the constantly evolving world of sport. That is how FLCS Clubhouse was born, says Tomas Van Den Spiegel, CEO of Flanders Classics: “We give each of our races a recognisable identity, and with FLCS Clubhouse we now want to build further on Flanders Classics’ identity as a company. It is a concept that perfectly matches the kind of company we want to be: an innovative organiser that places community and experiences for fans and active athletes at the heart of what we do.” The 5-day programme is now live.

item.title: The undisputed king of Willunga Hill
item.description: Richie Porte’s induction into the Santos Tour Down Under Hall of Fame this year celebrates a career defined by dominance on South Australian soil. A two-time overall winner in 2017 and 2020, and four-time runner-up, he also claimed eight individual stage victories, including his legendary streak up Willunga Hill every year from 2014 to 2019.

item.title: Richie Porte, the undisputed king of Willunga Hill
item.description: Richie Porte’s induction into the Santos Tour Down Under Hall of Fame this year celebrates a career defined by dominance in South Australia. He was a two-time overall winner in 2017 and 2020, a four-time runner-up, and won eight individual stages, including his famous streak on Willunga Hill every year from 2014 to 2019.

item.description: On 27 March, we are offering 500 free fries again thanks to Agristo from 1 p.m. in the E3 Arena. After the 500 free fries, they will be sold and the proceeds will go to the charity Het Ventiel. #E3SaxoClassic #VlaanderensSpectaculairste

item.title: Race day means atmosphere: 500 free fries at the E3 Saxo Arena on 27 March
item.description: On 27 March, we are once again offering 500 free fries from 1 p.m. at the E3 Saxo Arena, thanks to Agristo. It is a tasty initiative that will make the race atmosphere in Harelbeke even warmer for supporters, volunteers and visitors. After the 500 free fries, they will be sold and the proceeds will go to the charity vzw Het Ventiel. #E3SaxoClassic #VlaanderensSpectaculairste

item.title: Paris-Nice ends with Marc Soler in the top 10 on the final day in Nice
item.description: That’s the end of Paris-Nice for another year, and we gave it our all. On the final day in Nice, Marc Soler launched a long-range attack. In the end, Marc finished in the top 10 to secure sixth place overall. Great job this week, guys. We kept fighting.

item.title: GreenEDGE team finishes Paris-Nice with three riders
item.description: That wraps up a tough edition of Paris-Nice for us. Our three surviving riders made it to the finish.

item.title: NSN Cycling Team wraps up Paris-Nice
item.description: Wrapping up the Race to the Sun in the sun. Another day of hard racing in the legs for our riders at Paris-Nice.

item.title: Rudy Molard finishes 15th on the final stage of Paris-Nice
item.description: At Paris-Nice, Rudy Molard finished 15th on the final stage in Nice. In the general classification, Rémi Cavagna held on to 16th place overall.

item.title: Harold Tejada finishes third in Nice at Paris-Nice
item.description: Harold Tejada took third place in Nice at Paris-Nice.

item.title: Paris-Nice: the first race in France in our new colours
item.description: Paris-Nice was our first race on French soil in our new colours. It was Ion’s last race in the sun. In the end, we finished seventh overall and had five top-10 stage results. Above all, it was a collective mindset, with the desire to fight every day. We wanted to look back on this special week from a different angle. Video and text by @mathildelazou.

item.title: Jonas Vingegaard reflects on his Paris-Nice victory
item.description: "A stage win would have been the icing on the cake, but I’m extremely happy that I was able to secure the overall victory." - Jonas Vingegaard. Relive Jonas’s triumph in the race report.

item.title: Marc Soler finishes sixth overall at Paris-Nice
item.description: He attacked on the final day of Paris-Nice, but it was not enough for a long-range raid. Marc Soler stepped up in the absence of team leader Brandon McNulty and rode a consistent week to finish sixth overall.

item.title: Paris-Nice 2026 jerseys and awards after the race
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard won the yellow jersey, green jersey, and polka dot jersey at Paris-Nice 2026. Georg Steinhauser won the white jersey. Valentin Paret-Peintre won the combativity prize. Ineos Grenadiers was the best team. Lenny Martinez won a stage.

item.title: Paris-Nice 2026 jerseys and awards after the race
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard won the yellow jersey, green jersey, and polka dot jersey at Paris-Nice 2026. Georg Steinhauser won the white jersey. Valentin Paret-Peintre won the combativity prize. Ineos Grenadiers was the best team. Lenny Martinez won a stage.

item.title: Michał Kwiatkowski on Paris-Nice
item.description: Michał Kwiatkowski said it was the best team, with two stage wins and a strong effort until the end. He said it was a great couple of days with the team at Paris-Nice and tagged INEOS Grenadiers.

item.title: Classifications after stage 8 of Paris-Nice
item.description: After stage 8 of Paris-Nice: Lenny Martinez won the stage, Valentin Paret-Peintre won the combativity prize, Jonas Vingegaard led the general classification, the King of the Mountains classification, and the points classification, and Georg Steinhauser led the white jersey classification. Ineos Grenadiers was the best team.

item.title: Exceptional giveaway: win a signed LCL yellow jersey from Jonas Vingegaard
item.description: Exceptional giveaway: what if the next Paris-Nice 2026 winner was already at your home? Win a yellow LCL jersey signed by Jonas Vingegaard. A unique keepsake for all cycling fans. To enter: 1) Follow @sev_dacosta, @maillotjaunelcl, and @parisnicecourse 2) Like and save this post 3) Tag 2 or 3 cycling friends in the comments. One tagged friend = one extra chance. Bonus: share this post in your story and mention @sev_dacosta to double your chances. The draw will take place very soon.

item.title: A huge battle for the overall win
item.description: Isaac del Toro and Giulio Pellizzari put in massive efforts in their fight for the Tirreno-Adriatico overall victory. The Mexican stayed calm when the Italian attacked, timing his move to perfection to take the stage and extend his overall lead. Isaac del Toro: Time 2'56", speed 16.6 km/h, max speed 30.1 km/h, power 530 W, max power 800 W. Giulio Pellizzari: Time 3'18", speed 15.1 km/h, max speed 29.0 km/h, power 490 W, max power 790 W.

item.title: ORLEN VITAY Award for Best Polish Rider returns at Tour de Pologne
item.description: The ORLEN VITAY Award for Best Polish Rider will again be awarded during the Tour de Pologne. On each stage of the 83rd edition, the Polish rider with the best result will receive the ORLEN VITAY Best Polish Rider prize. Last year, Rafał Majka (UAE Team Emirates - XRG) led this classification, finishing as the best Polish rider on the stages to Karpacz, Zakopane and Bukowina Tatrzańska, as well as in the individual time trial around the Wieliczka Salt Mine. In the sprint finishes in Legnica and Cieszyn, Stanisław Aniołkowski (Cofidis) was the fastest Polish rider. Cheer for the Polish riders during the 83rd Tour de Pologne. @orlenteam_official #ORLEN #TdP 📸 @szymon_gruchalski_sports

item.title: ORLEN VITAY Award for Best Polish Rider returns at Tour de Pologne
item.description: The ORLEN VITAY Award for Best Polish Rider will again be awarded during the Tour de Pologne. Last year, Rafał Majka (UAE Team Emirates - XRG) led this classification, finishing as the best Polish rider on the stages to Karpacz, Zakopane and Bukowina Tatrzańska, as well as in the individual time trial around the Wieliczka Salt Mine. In the sprint finishes in Legnica and Cieszyn, Stanisław Aniołkowski (Cofidis) was the fastest Polish rider. Cheer for the Polish riders during the 83rd Tour de Pologne. item.title: From bike to helicopter @gregvanavermaet
item.description: @gregvanavermaet is known for the cobbles and climbs of the @e3_saxoclassic. This year, he is watching the race from our cockpit. In our latest video, Greg takes an introductory flight with Toran and discovers what it feels like to pilot a helicopter over the E3 course. During the E3, you can experience it too with Greg. Join a flight over Harelbeke and see the course from the air. 📅 Friday 27 March 📍 Forestiersstadion, Harelbeke #e3saxoclassic #gregvanavermaet #helikopter #luchtdoop

item.title: Who will be crowned King of the HLN E3 Col?
item.description: Who will be crowned King of the HLN E3 Col? We had the honor of guiding Olympic champion Greg Van Avermaet on his KOM attempt. A legendary rider and a memorable moment for us. He still has it. We challenge you to try to match Greg’s time in this challenge, in collaboration with HLN and the E3. Go for it in true King’s style.

item.title: Tour of Britain 2025: Women’s Stage 1 recap
item.description: With the 2026 races fast approaching, we’re looking back stage by stage at how the 2025 Tour of Britain unfolded across both the men’s and women’s races. We start with Women’s Stage 1, where Kim Le Court wasted no time making her mark, taking three jerseys on day one, while Cat Ferguson secured the Young Rider jersey.

item.title: Who will be king of the E3-Col?
item.description: This is your chance to prove yourself against the pro peloton and former riders. HLN, the Kings of the Flemish Mountains, and the E3 Saxo Classic challenge you: can you climb faster than Mathieu van der Poel, Wout van Aert, Tadej Pogacar, Greg Van Avermaet, or Jan Bakelants? Take part in our challenge and win an exclusive place on the podium of the E3 Saxo Classic on 27 March.

item.title: Exclusive helicopter baptism at E3 Saxo Classic on Friday 27 March 2026
item.description: On Friday 27 March 2026, we offer an exclusive Abutriek Services helicopter baptism during E3 Saxo Classic. In 6 minutes, you can experience the region around Harelbeke from a Robinson R44, a short but unforgettable extra experience on race day. Book your places via https://lnkd.in/eMZpmqNc, first come, first served. #E3SaxoClassic #VlaanderensSpectaculairste

item.title: Strade Bianche is more than a race
item.description: #ADV A year ago I was alone, out in the middle of nowhere, riding toward North Cape. Today I am no longer alone. Strade Bianche is not just a race. It is an atmosphere: the lively city, the teams getting ready, the anticipation before the peloton passes. The Fan Zone at Le Tolfe, Siena, the music, the toasts, and that feeling that something special is about to happen. The peloton passes in ten seconds, but the day stays with you. Last year I was riding alone. Today I am riding inside an emotion shared with thousands of people. @strade_bianche #StradeBianche

Flanders Classics and Playground join forces to grow endurance events in France and the Benelux
item.description: Flanders Classics and Playground are joining forces to accelerate the development of endurance events in France and the Benelux region. By combining Playground’s expertise in running events with the cycling know-how of Flanders Classics, the partnership aims to further grow running in the Benelux and cycling in France. Tomas Van Den Spiegel, CEO of Flanders Classics, said that Playground shares their vision of demanding, authentic events rooted in their territories. He added that the partnership will help combine expertise to create new formats, reach new communities, and support the sustainable development of running and cycling across Europe.
