item.title: From Antwerp to Oudenaarde: the Ronde van Vlaanderen experience
item.description: From the buzzing start in Antwerp to the iconic finish in Oudenaarde, the Ronde van Vlaanderen is more than just a race, it is Flemish cycling heritage. Do you want to feel the thunder of the peloton and see the decisive moves from the best seats in the house? Our VIP programme takes you deep into the heart of Flanders’ Finest. Experience the Ronde like never before. #FLCS #SetThePace #RVV26

item.title: Tadej Pogačar wins Strade Bianche for the fourth time
item.description: Tadej Pogačar cannot be beaten. The Slovenian takes his fourth victory in Strade Bianche. In the women’s race, Elise Chabbey claims a surprise win. Best analysis of the race: go.ivoox.com/rf/169692024. Official podcast: El Maillot.

item.title: A huge ride from 19-year-old Paul Seixas
item.description: Paul Seixas confirmed his talent with a brilliant second place at Strade Bianche, behind Tadej Pogačar and ahead of Isaac del Toro. Paul Seixas’s 77.4 km chase of Pogačar: Time 1h56'08", average speed 39.8 km/h, max speed 88.9 km/h, average power 330 W, max power 930 W.

item.title: Power from another planet
item.description: Tadej Pogačar produced extraordinary numbers during his 77.4 km attack to victory at Strade Bianche. He went solo with about 76.4 km to go. Tadej Pogačar’s 77.4 km winning attack: Time 1h55'07", average speed 40.2 km/h, max speed 90.7 km/h, average power 380 W, max power 810 W. Paul Seixas’s 77.4 km chase of Pogačar: Time 1h56'08", average speed 39.8 km/h, max speed 88.9 km/h, average power 330 W, max power 930 W.

item.title: Ronde van Vlaanderen Fan Ride: ride the final 100 km on race day
item.description: On Ronde van Vlaanderen day, 100 riders can ride the final 100 km, cheered on by thousands of fans. Before the pros tackle the hills and cobbles, you can ride the same legendary final 100 km in the official Ronde van Vlaanderen Fan Ride. Experience the iconic climbs, the support of the fans, and the finish line just hours before the world’s best riders. Only a few spots remain in this unique event.

item.title: Tour de Suisse 2026 stage locations, partnerships, and race development
item.description: Preparations for the 2026 Tour de Suisse are well underway. With Locarno and Aarburg, the stage locations for this year’s national tour are now complete. At the same time, the organizers continue to develop the race sustainably and are strengthening the partner network, including Decathlon as official outfitter.

item.title: Matthew Brennan makes history at Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne 2026
item.description: On Sunday 1 March 2026, Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne delivered another thrilling edition. After a hectic and attacking race, 20-year-old British rider Matthew Brennan sprinted to his first victory in a Flemish Classic, a win that reflects the identity of Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne: Where Legends Are Born.

item.title: Simon Defrance wins Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne Juniors 2026
item.description: On Sunday 1 March 2026, Kuurne once again showcased the stars of tomorrow. After an animated race, French rider Simon Defrance claimed victory in the 35th edition of Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne Juniors, a result that reflects the race motto: Where Legends Are Born.

item.title: E3 Saxo Classic 2026 route update: even more racing in the finale
item.description: Route update for the E3 Saxo Classic 2026: there will be even more racing in the finale. This year, the riders will cross the Oude Kwaremont twice, and the E3-Col will play an even more central role in the race. The aim is a race that opens up from far out, where positioning, timing, and courage make the difference. Friday 27 March 2026 in Harelbeke.

item.title: The Ecuadorian champion, @jhonatan_narvaez97, turns 29 today
item.description: The Ecuadorian champion, @jhonatan_narvaez97, turns 29 today. After making an impressive first appearance at the Santos Tour Down Under in 2024 to take second place, he returned a year later with @uaeteamemirates to claim the 2025 overall title. A historic moment as the first Ecuadorian to win the GC in South Australia. Happy birthday, Jhonny!

item.title: The Ecuadorian champion, Jhonatan Narváez, turns 29 today
item.description: The Ecuadorian champion, Jhonatan Narváez, turns 29 today. After making an impressive first appearance at the Santos Tour Down Under in 2024 to take second place overall, he returned a year later with UAE Team Emirates to claim the 2025 overall title. A historic moment as the first Ecuadorian to win the GC in South Australia. Happy birthday, Jhonny!

item.title: The Ecuadorian champion, Jhonatan Narváez, turns 29 today
item.description: The Ecuadorian champion, Jhonatan Narváez, turns 29 today. After making an impressive first appearance at the Santos Tour Down Under in 2024 to take second place overall, he returned a year later with @TeamEmiratesUAE to claim the 2025 overall title. A historic moment as the first Ecuadorian to win the GC in South Australia. Happy birthday, Jhonny! — Santos Tour Down Under 🚴🚴‍♀️ (@tourdownunder) Mar 4, 2026

item.title: During Volunteer Week, we want to thank all our Kuurne-Brussel-Kuurne volunteers
item.description: During Volunteer Week, we want to thank all our Kuurne-Brussel-Kuurne volunteers who help make our spring classic a top event. We are also looking for enthusiastic volunteers for the weekend and committed people to strengthen our board (VIP cell, sales cell, or logistics cell). Whether you can help for a few hours during the weekend or want to contribute more structurally in one of our teams, all help is welcome. Interested? Send your application to info@kbk.be. Together, we will make next year another great edition. #WeekVanDeVrijwilliger #KBK

item.title: The 2026–2027 UCI Cyclo-cross World Cup calendar is now complete
item.description: The 2026–2027 Union Cycliste Internationale Cyclo-cross World Cup calendar is now officially complete as Glasgow, Scotland secures the final spot and will host round three in Kelvingrove Park on 13 December 2026. Tomas Van Den Spiegel, CEO of Flanders Classics, said: "We have been working on bringing a UCI Cyclo-cross World Cup event to Great Britain for several years and are delighted to be able to announce Glasgow for next winter. Introducing a new venue is always special, especially in a major city like Glasgow, and we are looking forward to a great collaboration with British Cycling." See you in December! #FLCS #SetThePace

item.title: Our official E3 Saxo Classic retro collection is live
item.description: Our official E3 Saxo Classic retro collection is live. With this collection, we pay tribute to our history, translated into items that cycling fans will love to wear on and off the cobbles. We are proud to activate this again in collaboration with AD Delhaize Harelbeke. Through a local stamp campaign, supporters can collect exclusive gadgets at a discounted price. This connects cycling, community, and retail in an accessible way. The full collection is also available in our webshop: https://e3webshop.be #E3SaxoClassic #VlaanderensSpectaculairste

item.description: This is where the story was written. The heroes faced wind, distance, mountains, and tough conditions. From a relentless desert to sky-high peaks, #طواف_الإمارات was more than a race for excellence and glory. Every stage told a story, every kilometer was a challenge, and every finish line marked the start of a new dream.

item.title: The Spring Classics season has officially begun
item.description: The Spring Classics season has officially begun. Last Saturday, Omloop Nieuwsblad opened the road cycling spring in style between Ghent and Ninove. Mathieu van der Poel and Demi Vollering won the first cobbled Classic of the year. Mathieu van der Poel controlled the race in his first Omloop Nieuwsblad and turned a selective finale into a decisive victory on the Muur-Kapelmuur. On the women’s side, Demi Vollering rode a smart race with perfect timing, attacking in Geraardsbergen and finishing with a strong sprint after riding clear with Katarzyna Niewiadoma. It was also her first victory in Ninove. #FLCS #SetThePace

item.title: Copenhagen Sprint opens opportunities for business partners
item.description: Copenhagen Sprint is offering companies the chance to join the event as a public partner through a mobile advertising caravan along the circuit in Copenhagen. The caravan will ride the course and stop at selected hot spots, creating atmosphere with sound and activity. During this time, there will be no other activity on the route, and it will run as close as possible to the professional field. As a public partner, your brand will be displayed in the city through cargo bikes and you will be part of the Copenhagen Sprint setup. Contact Mathias Pedersen at mape@cyklingdanmark.dk for more information.

item.title: Vollering beats Niewiadoma-Phinney at the finish
item.description: After a decisive acceleration on the Muur van Geraardsbergen, the opening classic was decided in a two-rider sprint. European champion Demi Vollering took the victory.

item.title: Mathieu van der Poel solos to victory in Ninove
item.description: Mathieu van der Poel made an immediate statement in his first participation in Omloop het Nieuwsblad. The Dutchman accelerated on the Muur van Geraardsbergen and rode solo to victory in Ninove.

item.title: Volta ao Algarve through the sunny roads of southern Portugal
item.description: Along the sunny roads of southern Portugal, crossing agricultural plains and mountain roads, sometimes cobbled or heated by the tar, that was the Volta ao Algarve. With incredible landscapes and an enthusiastic crowd, it was about 700 km of sweat, suffering, joy and companionship. A parade of stars, from WorldTeams to Portuguese continental teams, delighted the fans, who were able to enjoy the close access that cycling allows. Algarve, Portugal © 2026 Igor Martins / FPC #voltaaoalgarve #fpc #ciclismo #sonyalpha #sonyportugal

item.title: Flanders Classics and KPMG Belgium extend their collaboration on Closing The Gap
item.description: Flanders Classics and KPMG Belgium are extending their collaboration within the Closing The Gap project, reaffirming a shared commitment to sustainable and equal women’s cycling. With the category upgrades of Dwars door Vlaanderen and Scheldeprijs, all women’s races will ultimately reach the same competition level as their male equivalents in 2026. In recent years, not only has the professional peloton benefited from the FLCS Women project, but registrations among girls at the Tour of Flanders Youth Day have doubled as well. Building on this momentum, the partnership will continue over the next three years to further strengthen the future of women’s cycling. Growing women’s cycling further internationally also means helping to build it from the grassroots up. With rapid professionalisation, there is a need for a strong and sustainable inflow of young talent. Tomas Van Den Spiegel: "In 2022, at the first Tour of Flanders Youth Day, the girls U17 and U19 cycled together due to the lower number of entries. But for the upcoming fifth edition, no fewer than 45 girls U19 teams have applied, of which we can accept only 25. It’s a clear signal of growth and potential. In this way, we are also doing our bit to close the gap, not only at the professional top but also at the grassroots level.” #FLCS #SetThePace

item.title: A week at the Volta ao Algarve with the fastest riders in the world
item.description: A week at the Volta ao Algarve with the fastest riders in the world, alongside riders I had only ever watched on TV. The Algarve did not disappoint: stunning landscapes, crowds lining the roads, music, energy, and iconic places like Alte, the perfect backdrop for any cycling photographer. Paul Magnier dominated the sprints, winning in Tavira and Lagos. Paul Seixas made history at Alto da Fóia with his first professional victory at just 19, on the same climb where Tadej Pogačar won his first pro race in 2019. Filippo Ganna powered through the Vilamoura time trial, showing incredible strength, and Juan Ayuso conquered Malhão to take the overall win. Paul, Paul, Paul: Magnier. Paul, Paul, Paul: Seixas. Filippo, Filippo: Ganna. Juan, Juan, Juan: Ayuso. A week at the heart of the race, living the life of a WorldTour photographer: learning, connecting, and feeling genuinely happy. Thanks to my partner in crime, @im.igor.martins, everyone involved, the partner teams, and the @fedportciclismo for always trusting me. Cycling gives me so much. I try to give back in the best way I can. It was unforgettable.

item.title: Proximus Group and Flanders Classics extend partnership
item.description: In modern cycling, being connected is everything. That is why we are happy to announce the extended partnership between Proximus Group and Flanders Classics. From high-speed data for our production teams to fans streaming the Paterberg climb on their phones, Proximus helps bring the pulse of the Flemish Spring Classics to a global audience. Together, we are not just celebrating the race, we are enhancing the digital experience of every fan, rider, and partner involved. Think possible. #FLCS #SetThePace

item.title: Copenhagen Sprint volunteer opportunity in Roskilde
item.description: Last Friday we attended the Roskilde Idræts Union sports festival, where local athletes were honored. We wanted to share the opportunity to become a volunteer for Copenhagen Sprint, which starts in Roskilde, where the team and rider presentation also takes place. We are very excited to kick off Copenhagen Sprint in Roskilde Municipality on 13-14 June. Learn more about volunteering here: https://lnkd.in/eTiKRvKv

item.title: UAE Tour 2026: Bin Hamoodah Auto Stage 1 route overview
What will your favorites ride in the Tour de Pologne 2026?
item.description: When the peloton rolls off the start line, we see the leaders’ jerseys and the faces of champions. But just beneath them is the race’s second hero: the bike. WorldTour 2026 machines are true technological beasts: minimum 6.8 kg, wireless shifting, carbon wheels, aerodynamic frames, and prices ranging from 60,000 PLN to several hundred thousand PLN for a complete bike. How different are they from a regular high-end bike? Why do some teams have to add weight to their bikes? And how much is technology, and how much is still the rider’s power? Read more in our latest article. #ORLEN #TourdePologne #WorldTour #TdP2026

item.title: I spent many beautiful years of my life in Italy.
item.description: I spent many beautiful years of my life in Italy. It is a country that gave me sporting maturity, experience, and lifelong friendships. 🇮🇹 But no matter where I am, my heart always beats Polish. 🇵🇱 I am deeply moved when I see the presence of Polish brands and organizations at international events. Every meeting with their representatives is more than a conversation: it is warmth, kindness, and the special emotions that come with meeting a fellow countryman abroad. At the Winter Olympic Games, our @polskaorganizacjaturystyczna is also present - an institution that has for years been building Poland as a brand in the world in a remarkably consistent and modern way. It is a partner with whom I have worked for many years in organizing Tour de Pologne. Together we show that sport is one of the most beautiful ambassadors of our country. @tourdepologne is now not only a cycling race. It is a global platform for promoting Poland. As part of the elite UCI World Tour, we host the world's best teams, and through 5 hours of uninterrupted television coverage we reach more than 65 countries. In that time, the world sees not only top-level competition, but also our cities, regions, landscapes, and the energy of our people. This is enormous promotional value and proof that we can organize events at a truly world-class level. Poland is increasingly and more boldly making its presence felt on the international stage - through sport, tourism, and the professional organization of major events. And I will tell you one thing: when I see the white and red colors among the world elite, I feel great joy and pride. Because Poland truly has much to be proud of! 🇵🇱✨ • tourism • Polish Tourist Organisation • proud of Poland • House of Poland • Winter Olympic Games • Milan • Cortina

item.title: Gdynia celebrates 100 years of city rights. The 83rd Tour de Pologne will start there on 3 August
item.description: Gdynia celebrates 100 years of city rights today. It is in this unique port city that the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour will start on 3 August. Gdynia, a symbol of a modern city and the achievements of the Second Polish Republic, has been developing dynamically for 100 years and continues to write new chapters in its history. In 2026, the world’s best cycling teams will be seen on Kościuszko Square. “Gdynia was a child of an independent, sovereign Republic, built on hard work, sweat, sacrifice, and beautiful ideals,” said Karol Nawrocki, President of the Republic of Poland, today during a ceremonial session of the City Council. The return of the Tour de Pologne to Gdynia is symbolic—the race last visited the city in 2004. The 83rd edition will take place from 3–9 August 2026. Gdynia. History. Tour de Pologne. Cycling #ORLEN

item.title: Gdynia celebrates 100 years of city rights. This is where the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour will begin
item.description: Today marks a special anniversary: the 100th anniversary of Gdynia being granted city rights. The port city, a symbol of modern development, will host the start of the 83rd Tour de Pologne UCI WorldTour on 3 August. In 2026, the world’s best cycling teams will be seen on Kościuszko Square. The race last visited Gdynia in 2004. The 83rd edition will take place from 3–9 August 2026.

item.title: The Best of UAE Tour Women 2026
Telenet Superprestige round 8 in Middelkerke: season finale recap
item.description: Last Saturday marked round 8 of the Telenet Superprestige, with Middelkerke hosting a thrilling final showdown. With both the Men Elite and Women Elite general classifications still wide open, everything was on the line. In the Men Elite standings, Niels Vandeputte started the day in the lead, with Michael Vanthourenhout just three points behind. The race was decided through perfect teamwork, as Vanthourenhout took the victory after strong support from teammate Gerben Kuypers. Vandeputte finished second, a result that was enough to secure the general classification for the second year in a row. The Women Elite race saw Amandine Fouquenet take control from start to finish. Riding with confidence, she built a comfortable lead and claimed her third victory of this year’s Telenet Superprestige. Ceylin Del Carmen Alvarado came close in the closing stages but had to settle for second place. Aniek van Alphen finished eighth, which proved just enough to secure the overall classification. She ended the series level on points with Fouquenet, but with more race participations, Van Alphen was crowned Telenet Superprestige champion. A remarkable season came to an end in Middelkerke. Thanks to all of our partners for their continued support throughout the season: Telenet group, AD Garage Belgium, Sunseeker Robotics, Isolatiestock BV, Alken-Maes, Electro Depot Belgium, Prik&Tik, ASSA ABLOY Opening Solutions, FrieslandCampina, Kärcher Belux, and Ethias. #FLCS #SetThePace

item.title: Could a cycling-focused audience be interesting for you? Take a look here.
item.description: We will have an expo area right in the middle of the Copenhagen Sprint event. The expo area will be placed right next to the route and will be a natural meeting point for cycling fans, families and curious passers-by. For exhibitors, this means visibility in an area with high energy, long dwell time and an audience open to dialogue, inspiration and new products. For the 2026 race, we have chosen Christian Ritter to be responsible for sales of stand spaces in the expo area. In addition to his work with Copenhagen Sprint, Christian Ritter is also behind Copenhagen Bike Show, Scandinavia's largest cycling fair. Interested? Contact Christian Ritter at christian@ritter.dk or 2042 0006. Read more here: https://lnkd.in/dZPH2rkQ

item.title: Food vendors wanted for Copenhagen Sprint 2026
item.description: In collaboration with Good Boys Agency, we are looking for passionate food vendors to serve meals at our cycling festival. Copenhagen Sprint once again combines world-class racing with a family-friendly cycling festival focused on sustainability, diversity, and community. We are looking for food concepts that reflect these values and contribute to an open, safe, and lively atmosphere. We are seeking vendors who work with sustainable choices and quality ingredients, offer food for many people including vegetarian, vegan, and child-friendly options, can provide efficient and welcoming service in a fast-paced event, have an attractive food stand that fits the event, and offer reasonable prices with at least one affordable option. Read the terms and conditions and complete the application form with a short description of your concept. After submission, we will respond to let you know whether we can offer you a place. Application deadline: 15 March 2026.

item.title: Tour de Suisse: A new beginning and 2026 stage locations
item.description: The Tour de Suisse 2026 marks the beginning of a new era in professional cycling. The women’s and men’s races will take place on the same day and at the same location, on circuit courses. This is a world first, with no comparable format yet at international level. Another first follows: for the first time, the stage locations for 2026 are being announced.

item.title: Simon Clarke retires after 17 years in the peloton
item.description: Seventeen years, 15 Santos Tour Down Under appearances, two Olympics, and three Grand Tour stage wins. Simon Clarke has officially retired from professional cycling, after one final appearance at the Santos Tour Down Under this year. Thank you, Simon.

One question remains: who will succeed Jasper Philipsen at the finish in Kuurne?

item.title: How can events contribute to the green transition?
item.description: This is something we focus on and work to build into the different stages of race planning. Last week, DanskHåndbold, Bæredygtig Herning and Sport Event Denmark hosted an inspiring seminar on how sport events can move in the right direction when it comes to the green transition. There we shared some of the initiatives we are working on in Copenhagen Sprint. When federations, event organizers, host cities, athletes and commercial partners work together, we can not only help each other succeed, we can move an entire industry in a greener direction, one pedal stroke at a time.

item.title: The 2026 Season Starts This Saturday at the Santos Women’s Tour Down Under
item.description: The 2026 season starts this Saturday. On January 17, the professional women’s peloton opens the new season at the Santos Women’s Tour Down Under in Australia. Two Polish riders are on the start line: Dominika Włodarczyk (UAE Team ADQ), who was 4th overall last year, and Marta Lach (Team SD Worx – Protime). The race features three stages around Adelaide, punchy climbs, and the decisive Corkscrew Road finale. Read more on our website at tourdepolognewomen.pl/en/.

item.title: Copenhagen Sprint 2026 Set for June 13-14
item.description: Denmark has something unique: committed municipalities and authorities actively take part in planning major sports and cultural events. Yesterday, we held a joint meeting with all the municipalities and authorities involved in Copenhagen Sprint. It was a pleasure to see the support from so many participants, and we do not take that for granted. Thank you all. The purpose of the day was to strengthen cooperation across municipal borders and create a shared direction for the race and its organization. Copenhagen Sprint 2026 will take place on June 13-14, and you can already look forward to a fantastic weekend of cycling. Roskilde Municipality, Frederikssund Municipality, Fredensborg Municipality, Ballerup Municipality, Copenhagen Municipality, Rødovre Municipality, Allerød Municipality, Egedal Municipality, Hillerød Municipality, Furesø Municipality, Frederiksberg Municipality, Copenhagen Police.

item.description: British rider Simon Yates has unexpectedly announced the end of his professional career. The cyclist representing Team Visma | Lease a Bike has decided to retire.

item.title: Congratulations to Simon Yates on an outstanding career
item.description: Congratulations to Simon Yates on an outstanding career in the peloton, with highlights including overall victory at both the Vuelta a España and the Giro d’Italia, multiple Grand Tour stage wins, gold in the points race at the 2013 UCI Track World Championships, and selection to represent Team GB at the Tokyo 2020 Olympic Games. All the best for whatever comes next, Simon 🚴‍♂️💙 📸 Pics 1-7: SWpix.com 📸 Pic 8: Arne Mill/SWpix.com

item.title: How do you build a partnership that lasts for generations?
item.description: Brouwerij De Brabandere talks about nearly 70 years of working with E3 Saxo Classic: shared values, including character, authenticity and local roots, plus an experience that resonates with both fans and guests. With Kwaremont as a natural fit for Vlaanderens Spectaculairste. If your company wants to make a difference, more info via https://lnkd.in/egyZjjEp or email jacques@e3saxoclassic.be #E3SaxoClassic #VlaanderensSpectaculairste #DeBrabandere #Kwaremont

item.title: Volunteers needed for Copenhagen Sprint 2026
item.description: When Copenhagen Sprint returns on 13–14 June 2026, it will again rely on dedicated volunteers. Associations and clubs play a central role. The organizers are looking for clubs and associations willing to help as flag marshals, spectator guides, and in other key roles along the route.

item.title: GPCQM documentary reruns on TVA Sports during the holidays
item.description: If you missed GPCQM – Beyond the Finish Line on TVA Sports, reruns are scheduled throughout the holiday season. Relax with a hot chocolate and relive the highlights of the 14th edition. Tune in on TVA Sports on 21, 23, and 25 December 2025, as well as 1 and 2 January 2026.

item.title: Matteo Trentin wins Paris-Tours again after ten years
item.description: Ten years after his first win in Paris-Tours, Matteo Trentin did it again. He is the only rider to have won both versions of the race and is now a three-time winner.

item.title: News about Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne 2026
item.description: Latest news from Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne 2026, including elite, general, juniors, and VIP updates. On Sunday 1 March 2026, Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne delivered another thrilling edition. After a hectic and attacking race, 20-year-old British talent Matthew Brennan sprinted to his first victory in a Flemish Classic.

item.title: Omloop het Nieuwsblad start to remain in Ghent until 2031
item.description: The start of Omloop het Nieuwsblad will remain in Ghent for the 2026–2031 period. Flanders Classics has reached an agreement with the City of Ghent.

item.title: Pogačar and Reusser to line up at the Tour de Suisse 2026
item.description: After Tadej Pogačar's participation was announced last weekend, another major name has now been confirmed: defending champion and ITT World Champion Marlen Reusser will return to the Tour de Suisse in 2026. The Swiss rider dominated the previous edition with an impressive performance, securing the overall victory convincingly.

item.title: Tour de Suisse 2026 looks set to be a major event
item.description: Tour de Suisse says it is looking forward to the first participation of Tadej Pogačar in 2026, a rider who shapes races and makes history.

item.description: Tuur Verbeeck wins KBK juniors 2025 with a strong solo ride. The 17-year-old Belgian attacks on the Brugsesteenweg and makes history.

item.description: Jasper Philipsen wins the mass sprint at KBK 2025. The Belgian sprint specialist strikes on his birthday on the Brugsesteenweg.

item.title: Good news: we will also present the UCI Road World Championships in Montreal in 2026
item.description: In addition to broadcasting the @grandsprixcyclistes in 2026, we will also present the UCI Road World Championships in Montreal. 11 September: Grand Prix Cycliste de Québec. 13 September: Grand Prix Cycliste de Montréal. UCI Road World Championships Montreal 2026: 20 September, elite women's individual time trial, 39.2 km; elite men's individual time trial, 39.2 km; 26 September, elite women's road race, 180.4 km; 27 September, elite men's road race, 273.7 km.

item.description: Tour de Pologne Women returns to the Lublin Region and will take place on July 24–26. From next season, it will join the UCI ProSeries.

item.title: ORLEN Tour de Pologne Amatorów 2025 summary
The ORLEN Lang Team Race 2025 series is over
Official kick-off for E3 Saxo Classic 2026 with partners
item.description: This week we brought our partners together for the official kick-off of E3 Saxo Classic, ahead of March 27, 2026. At Panoramix by Callens, our partners discovered the latest VIP and hospitality packages, networked, and listened to an inspiring keynote by Thomas Van der Plaetsen about hope, setbacks, and victories in sport and beyond. Thanks to all attending partners for their trust and the great conversations. Together, we will make E3 Saxo Classic 2026 a unique experience again, both along and on the course. If you want to be there live on March 27, 2026 with your partners or clients, discover and book our VIP and hospitality packages.

item.title: Copenhagen Sprint shortlisted for the Host City Awards 2025
item.description: Copenhagen Sprint has been shortlisted for the Host City Awards 2025 in the category of Excellence in Infrastructure & Delivery. We are very proud to be recognized. Thanks to all partners and collaborators who make Copenhagen Sprint possible. Being shortlisted in our very first year shows that the ambition is being noticed internationally.

The partnership combines the energy, performance, and emotions of professional cycling.

item.title: Spot Cross Atapuerca 2025
GPCQM – Stories from the Peloton premieres November 1 on CBC Gem
item.description: Discover GPCQM – Stories from the Peloton on November 1 on CBC Gem. Go behind the scenes of the Grands Prix Cyclistes de Québec et de Montréal, North America’s only UCI WorldTour races. This immersive documentary explores the athletes’ emotions, the organizers’ challenges, and the energy of the crowds that make these events unique. It also builds anticipation for the UCI Road World Championships Montréal 2026. The English version airs on CBC on November 1 at 2:30 p.m. The French version will air on TVA Sports on December 7.

item.title: More than 250,000 spectators in the first year
item.description: Interest in major international sporting events is strong in Denmark, according to the evaluation of Copenhagen Sprint prepared by the analysis institute Wilke. As many as 250,000 spectators attended in the first year.

item.title: Summary of the 20th Circuito Ciclocross Burgos 2025
A few weeks ago, the 2025 edition of the @grandsprixcyclistes took place, and we organized a contest...
item.description: A few weeks ago, the 2025 edition of the @grandsprixcyclistes took place, and we organized a contest to win a JURA machine. Here is the lucky winner of the S8, and we wish them many great tastings. Thank you to everyone who took part, and see you next year.

item.title: Did you expect the finish? I think we all did 🙃
item.description: I love experiencing intense days of sport this closely, and I hope I managed to share my emotions with you through this reel. The feelings you get in a major race like @ilombardia are impossible to describe, and so are the fans on the roadside on the Boccola climb — it gives you goosebumps. And what about the photos you sent me? I truly love this community and the sport that brings us together in all its forms. Thank you @castellicycling and @illombardia. Experiencing it like this was incredible.

item.title: The WorldTour season ends at the Tour of Guangxi #TOG2025
item.description: The WorldTour season comes to an end at the Tour of Guangxi in China. On the final stage, @ivoliveira1 sprinted to fifth place, while @jhonatan_narvaez97 finished safely to secure third overall. Before heading to the podium, Jhony made one last stop in Nanning.

item.title: Looking back on an unforgettable 14th edition of the #GPCQM
item.description: Looking back on an unforgettable 14th edition of the #GPCQM. Incredible crowds, a new start/finish site in Quebec, and a great atmosphere. Thank you to everyone who made this edition memorable. See you in 2026! Video — @GPCQM Sep 29, 2025

item.title: After Kigali, the relay passes to @Montreal_2026
item.description: In September 2026, Quebec will experience a historic month for cycling with the #GPCQM and the UCI Road World Championships Montreal 2026. Photo credit: @YPmedias — @GPCQM Sep 28, 2025

item.title: Congratulations to Pierre Laflamme, first Canadian to sit on the UCI Management Committee
item.description: Congratulations to Pierre Laflamme, the first Canadian to sit on the UCI Management Committee. This is a major step for Canadian cycling and an important international recognition. Montréal 2026 congratulates Pierre Laflamme on his election to the UCI Management Committee, a historic milestone that brings Quebec and Canadian cycling to the international stage.

item.title: Copenhagen Sprint 2026 rescheduled to 13-14 June
item.description: Copenhagen Sprint will return in 2026 on new dates. Originally scheduled for 20-21 June, the race has been moved forward one week and will now take place on 13-14 June 2026.

item.title: Missing the Tour of Britain?
item.description: Raise your hands if you’re missing the Tour of Britain. Lloyds Tour of Britain (@TourofBritain), Sep 25, 2025

item.title: Tour de Suisse 2026: Men and Women Racing in Parallel Over Five Days
item.description: From 17 to 21 June 2026, the Tour de Suisse will take place over five days, with two stages raced each day: one for men and one for women. Each stage will start and finish in the same location.

item.title: Tour de Suisse Evolves with Parallel Men’s and Women’s Racing
item.description: From 2026, the Tour de Suisse will be presented in a new format: over five days, two stages will be raced each day, one for men and one for women, starting and finishing in the same place.

item.title: Celebrating Another Incredible Year for the Lloyds Tour of Britain
item.description: Celebrating another incredible year for the Lloyds Tour of Britain. From record-breaking start lists to engaging content, the race brought the joy of cycling to more people than ever before and celebrated two retiring legends of British roads. Roll on 2026.

item.title: Remco Evenepoel Wins Third Straight World Championship Time Trial Title
item.description: The 2020 Tour de Pologne winner has claimed his third consecutive World Championship title in the individual time trial. On the demanding course in Kigali, Rwanda, the Soudal Quick-Step rider was unstoppable and finished more than a minute ahead of Jay Vine, with fellow Belgian Ilan Van Wilder taking third place. Evenepoel continues to make cycling history as a time trial specialist.

item.title: Thanks to LOTTO and @totalizator_sp, our Strategic Partner, for their continued support of the Tour de Pologne! We are glad to work together to develop Polish cycling and promote physical activity across the country. See you on the road! #WygrywamyRazem #tourdepologne #LOTTORadośćWygrywania #TotalizatorSportowy #PartnerStrategiczny

item.description: 🙌 MASTERCLASS 🙌 @tadejpogacar gives the win to his teammate @brandon_mcnulty after a perfect team race. At the end of a beautiful team effort, @brandon_mcnulty and @tadejpogacar take 1 and 2. #GPCQM

item.title: At 60 km to go, there is no longer a breakaway. Only a group of about 40 riders makes up the main group. The break is over. 40 riders are in the main group with 60 km to go. #GPCQM
item.description: At 60 km to go, there is no longer a breakaway. Only a group of about 40 riders makes up the main group. The break is over. 40 riders are in the main group with 60 km to go. #GPCQM

item.title: The chasers have joined the breakaway. There are now 15 riders at the front. #GPCQM
item.description: The chasers have joined the breakaway. There are now 15 riders at the front. #GPCQM

item.title: 3 riders are trying to join the breakaway / 3 riders are chasing: 🇬🇧 Lewis Askey 🇦🇺 Laurence Pithie 🇳🇴 Fredrik Dversnes Gap: 1'10 #GPCQM
item.description: 3 riders are trying to join the breakaway / 3 riders are chasing: 🇬🇧 Lewis Askey 🇦🇺 Laurence Pithie 🇳🇴 Fredrik Dversnes Gap: 1'10 #GPCQM

item.title: It is the teammates of defending champion Tadej Pogacar who are controlling the peloton. No attacks for now. Tadej Pogacar's teammates are leading the bunch. No attacks. #GPCQM
item.description: It is the teammates of defending champion Tadej Pogacar who are controlling the peloton. No attacks for now. Tadej Pogacar's teammates are leading the bunch. No attacks. #GPCQM

item.title: A morning breakaway has formed: 7 riders are up the road with 1 minute 20 seconds on the peloton.
item.description: A morning breakaway has formed. It includes 7 riders and has 1 minute 20 seconds on the peloton.

item.title: Grand Prix Cycliste de Montréal: the riders are off for 209 km.
item.description: Grand Prix Cycliste de Montréal: the riders have started the 209 km race.

item.title: More than a year after his last win, @alafpolak raises his arms again for the 45th victory of his career.
item.description: More than a year after his last victory, @alafpolak takes his 45th career win. Hats off, Julian. #GPCQM l 📸 @jstartt

item.description: Podium of the 2025 Grand Prix Cycliste de Québec: 1. @alafpolak1 2. @PavelSivakov 3. @AlbertoBettiol. Goosebumps! #GPCQM

item.title: The big winner: Mr. Alaphilippe.
item.description: The big winner: Mr. Alaphilippe. In control! A beast!

item.title: Last lap! 40 seconds between the breakaway and the peloton.
item.description: Last lap! 40 seconds between the breakaway and the peloton. It is going to be explosive. #GPCQM

item.title: 30 km to go: a big breakaway has formed, but the peloton is watching closely and remains 1 minute 16 seconds behind.
item.description: 30 km to go: a big breakaway has just formed, but the peloton is watching closely and remains 1 minute 16 seconds behind. We are preparing for the finish. #GPCQM

item.title: 37 km to go: the breakaway's lead is melting away as the pursuers and the peloton come back strongly.
item.description: 37 km to go: the breakaway's lead is shrinking fast. The pursuers and the peloton are coming back strong. It promises to be exciting. #GPCQM

item.title: 70 km to go: a large group of pursuers, including @NilsPolitt, @LaurencePithie and Julian Alaphilippe, attacks from the peloton.
item.description: 70 km to go: a large group of pursuers, including @NilsPolitt, @LaurencePithie and Julian Alaphilippe, wants to fight and gets away from the peloton. #GPCQM

item.title: Arnaud De Lie wins Leuven thriller to claim overall victory in the 20th Renewi Tour
item.description: A dramatic finale to the Renewi Tour saw Arnaud De Lie (Lotto Dstny) win the stage in Leuven and secure overall victory. For much of the day, Mathieu van der Poel looked set to take the general classification, but De Lie produced a decisive sprint in the closing metres.

Merlier wins second stage in Bilzen-Hoeselt, De Lie stays in yellow
item.description: Stage 4 of the Renewi Tour ended in another sprint finish, with European Champion Tim Merlier (Soudal–Quick Step) once again the fastest. On the uphill drag to Hoeselt, he came past Pavel Bittner and Olav Kooij to take his 14th victory of the season. Arnaud De Lie keeps the leader’s jersey by one second.

item.title: From Riemst to Bilzen-Hoeselt on day four
item.description: A fast day through the hills ended with another bunch sprint and another win for the European champion.

item.title: From Riemst to Bilzen-Hoeselt on day four
item.description: A fast day through the hills ended with another bunch sprint and another win for the European champion.

item.title: Van der Poel conquers Geraardsbergen, De Lie takes the leader’s jersey
item.description: After two sprint stages, the Renewi Tour tackled its queen stage in and around Geraardsbergen. On the iconic cobbled Vesten climb, Mathieu van der Poel (Alpecin-Deceuninck) outpowered Arnaud De Lie (Lotto Dstny) in a thrilling finale. Belgian champion Tim Wellens finished third. The race came to life early as the GC contenders took control.

item.description: Stage 2 of the Renewi Tour, raced in Belgium, delivered another bunch sprint despite crosswinds and repeated splits in the peloton. Tim Merlier wins opening stage and takes first leader's jersey in Terneuzen
item.description: The 2025 Renewi Tour began with a bunch sprint as expected, but the road to the finish was anything but straightforward. item.title: Giulio Ciccone wins the sprint, Jan Christen is best young rider, and Urko Berrade is the best Basque rider
item.description: Giulio Ciccone (@giuliocicco1) of @LidlTrek won the sprint classification. Jan Christen (@janchristen04) of @TeamEmiratesUAE was the best young rider. Urko Berrade (@Urkich) of @EqKernPharma was the best Basque rider. #Klasikoa2025

item.title: Giulio Ciccone wins the mountains classification at Klasikoa 2025
item.description: Podium: 1. Giulio Ciccone (@giuliocicco1) of @LidlTrek, 2. Jan Christen (@janchristen04) of @TeamEmiratesUAE, 3. Maxim Van Gils (@maximvangils) of @RBH_Procycling. Giulio Ciccone also won the mountains classification. #Klasikoa2025

item.title: Giulio Ciccone wins Klasikoa 2025
item.description: Final standings: 1. Giulio Ciccone (@ciccone_giulio) of @lidltrek, 2. Jan Christen (@janchristen04) of @uae_team_emirates, 3. Maxim Van Gils (@maximvangils) of @redbullborahansgrohe. Giulio Ciccone won the mountains and sprint classifications. Jan Christen was the best young rider. Urko Berrade (@urkoberrade) of @eqkernpharma was the best Basque rider. Team Visma | Lease a Bike won the team classification. Luke Plapp (@lukeplapp) of @greenedgecycling was the most combative rider. #Klasikoa2025

Final weekend in Vaduz postponed to 2027
item.description: The final weekend of the Tour de Suisse Men in Vaduz, originally planned for 2026, will be postponed by one year and will now take place in June 2027. Tour Director Olivier Senn explains the decision as follows: “The stage planning for 2026 and the associated logistical challenges have prompted us to request a postponement […]
